title.zh: 卷二十六 伏侯宋蔡馮趙牟韋列傳
title.en: Volume 26: Biographies of Fu, Hou, Song, Cai, Feng, Zhao, Mou, Wei
title.raw: 卷二十六 伏侯宋蔡馮趙牟韋列傳 Volume 26: Biographies of Fu, Hou, Song, Cai, Feng, Zhao, Mou, Wei

sentences.zh: 伏湛字惠公，瑯邪東武人也。

translations.literal: Fu Zhan, courtesy name Huigong, was a man of Dongwu in Langya.
translations.idiomatic: Fu Zhan, styled Huigong, was a native of Dongwu in Langya commandery.

sentences.zh: 九世祖勝，字子賤，所謂濟南伏生者也。

translations.literal: His ninth-generation ancestor Sheng, courtesy name Zijian, is the so-called Fu Sheng of Jinan.
translations.idiomatic: His ninth-generation ancestor Fu Sheng, styled Zijian, was the Old Master Fu of Jinan celebrated in the tradition.

sentences.zh: 湛高祖父孺，武帝時，客授東武，因家焉。

translations.literal: Zhan's great-great-grandfather Ru, in Emperor Wu's time, as a guest teacher lectured in Dongwu and thereupon made his home there.
translations.idiomatic: His great-great-grandfather Fu Ru taught in Dongwu as a visiting scholar under Emperor Wu and settled the family there.

sentences.zh: 父理，為當世名儒，以《詩》授成帝，為高密太傅，別自名學。

translations.literal: His father Li was a famed Confucian of the age; with the Odes he instructed Emperor Cheng; he was grand tutor in Gaomi and separately established a famed learning tradition.
translations.idiomatic: His father Fu Li was a celebrated scholar who taught Emperor Cheng the Odes, served as grand tutor in Gaomi, and founded a distinguished school of his own.

sentences.zh: 湛性孝友，少傳父業，教授數百人。

translations.literal: Zhan by nature was filial and friendly; young he transmitted his father's enterprise and taught several hundred.
translations.idiomatic: Filial and devoted to his brothers, he inherited his father's scholarship in youth and taught hundreds of students.

sentences.zh: 成帝時，以父任為博士弟子。

translations.literal: In Emperor Cheng's time, by his father's privilege he became a student of the erudite.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Cheng he entered the Academy as a student of the erudites by hereditary privilege.

sentences.zh: 五遷，至王莽時為繡衣執法，使督大奸，遷後隊屬正。

translations.literal: Five promotions—by Wang Mang's time he was embroidered-clothes law enforcer, sent to supervise great villains; promoted to adjutant of the Rear Army corps.
translations.idiomatic: After five promotions he served Wang Mang as an embroidered-robes inspector hunting major offenders, then rose to adjutant of the Rear Army corps.

sentences.zh: 更始立，以為平原太守。

translations.literal: When Gengshi was established, he was appointed grand administrator of Pingyuan.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Gengshi took the throne Fu Zhan became grand administrator of Pingyuan.

sentences.zh: 時倉卒兵起，天下驚擾，而湛獨晏然，教授不廢。

translations.literal: At that time hastily troops arose; the realm was alarmed and disturbed—but Zhan alone was tranquil; teaching did not cease.
translations.idiomatic: While rebellion threw the empire into panic, Fu alone stayed calm and never suspended instruction.

sentences.zh: 謂妻子曰：『夫一穀不登，國君徹膳；

translations.literal: He told wife and children, saying, "When one grain crop fails to ripen, the state's lord removes delicacies from meals;
translations.idiomatic: He told his household: "When one crop fails, the ruler sets aside fine foods;

sentences.zh: 今民皆饑，奈何獨飽？

translations.literal: " Now the people all hunger—how can we alone eat our fill?
translations.idiomatic: " Every household starves—how can we feast alone?"

sentences.zh: 』乃共食粗糲，悉分奉祿以賑鄉裏，來客者百餘家。

translations.literal: " Thereupon together they ate coarse grain; entirely dividing salary offerings to relieve village hamlets—those who came as guests were over a hundred households.
translations.idiomatic: " They ate coarse fare together and poured his official salary into feeding more than a hundred refugee households in the district.

sentences.zh: 時門下督素有氣力，謀欲為湛起兵，湛惡其惑眾，即收斬之，徇首城郭，以示百姓，於是吏人信向，郡向以安。

translations.literal: At that time the headquarters inspector always had martial vigor and plotted to raise troops for Zhan; Zhan hated his misleading the multitude—immediately seized and executed him, displayed heads along city walls to show the hundred clans—thereupon clerks and people trusted and inclined to him; the commandery inclined thereby to peace.
translations.idiomatic: A bold gate captain plotted rebellion in Fu's name; Fu had him executed at once and displayed his head on the walls to warn the people—officials and commoners then trusted him and the commandery grew calm.

sentences.zh: 平原一境，湛所全也。

translations.literal: The whole territory of Pingyuan—it was what Zhan preserved.
translations.idiomatic: Fu Zhan's stewardship spared all of Pingyuan.

sentences.zh: 光武即位，知湛名儒舊臣，欲信幹任內職，徵拜尚書，使典定舊制。

translations.literal: When Guangwu ascended the throne, knowing Zhan was a famed Confucian old minister, wishing to trust him with responsibility for inner offices—summoned and appointed Master of Writing—ordered him to supervise fixing old regulations.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu knew Fu as a veteran scholar-official and summoned him as Master of Writing to codify Han institutions.

sentences.zh: 時，大司徒鄧禹西征關中，帝以湛才任宰相，拜為司直，行大司徒事。

translations.literal: At this time Grand Minister of Education Deng Yu was west campaigning in Guanzhong; the emperor, considering Zhan's talents fit for chief minister—appointed him Director of Integrity to conduct Grand Minister of Education affairs.
translations.idiomatic: While Deng Yu campaigned in Guanzhong the emperor judged Fu equal to the chief ministership and named him Director of Integrity to stand in for the Grand Minister of Education.

sentences.zh: 車駕每出征伐，常留鎮守，總攝群司。

translations.literal: Whenever the imperial carriage went out on expeditions, he was constantly left to guard and secure—overall controlling the host of offices.
translations.idiomatic: Whenever the emperor marched he left Fu to guard Luoyang and oversee every ministry.

sentences.zh: 建武三年，遂代鄧禹為大司徒，封陽都侯。

translations.literal: In the third year of Jianwu he then replaced Deng Yu as Grand Minister of Education and was enfeoffed as Marquis of Yangdu.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 3 he succeeded Deng Yu as Grand Minister of Education with the title Marquis of Yangdu.

sentences.zh: 時，彭寵反於漁陽，帝欲自征之，湛上疏諫曰：『臣聞文王受命而征伐五國，必先詢之同姓，然後謀於群臣，加占蓍龜，以定行事，故謀則成，卜則吉，戰則勝。

translations.literal: At this time Peng Chong rebelled at Yuyang; the emperor wished personally to campaign against him; Zhan submitted a memorial remonstrating, saying, "Your servant has heard that when King Wen received the mandate and campaigned against five states, he first inquired of same-surname kin, then consulted among ministers, moreover divined by milfoil and tortoise to settle undertakings—therefore counsel succeeded, oracle was auspicious, battle won.
translations.idiomatic: When Peng Chong rebelled at Yuyang and Guangwu meant to lead the army himself, Fu memorialized: King Wen consulted kin and ministers and divined before every campaign—hence his victories.

sentences.zh: 其《詩》曰：「帝謂文王，詢爾仇方，同爾弟兄，以爾鉤援，與爾臨沖，以伐崇庸。

translations.literal: The Odes says: "God told King Wen: inquire of your hostile regions; unite your brothers; with your scaling-hooks and ropes; give you ram-battering rams and towers—to attack Chong's walls."
translations.idiomatic: The Odes records God charging King Wen to rally allies and kin and bring siege engines against Chong.

sentences.zh: 」崇國城守，先退後伐，所以重人命，俟時而動，故參分天下而有其二。

translations.literal: " Chong's state defended walls—first withdrew then attacked—thereby valuing human lives; waited for season then moved—therefore divided the realm in third parts yet possessed two of them.
translations.idiomatic: King Wen besieged Chong patiently—honoring lives and timing—until two-thirds of the realm leaned to Zhou.

sentences.zh: 陛下承大亂之極，受命而帝，興明祖宗，出入四年，而滅檀鄉，制五校，降銅馬，破赤眉，誅鄧奉之屬，不為無功。

translations.literal: Your Majesty inherits the utmost of great chaos—received mandate and became emperor—raised and enlightened ancestors and temples—in four years coming and going thus destroyed Tanxiang, restrained Five Camps, surrendered Bronze Horse, broke Red Eyebrows, executed Deng Feng's ilk—not without merit.
translations.idiomatic: You inherited utter chaos, restored the Liu temples, and in four years crushed Tanxiang, the Five Camps, the Bronze Horse, the Red Eyebrows, and Deng Feng—a formidable record.

sentences.zh: 今京師空匱，資用不足，未能服近而先事邊外；

translations.literal: Now the capital is empty and depleted; resources are insufficient—unable to subdue what is near yet first serving outer borders;
translations.idiomatic: The capital is drained—you cannot pacify the heartland yet rush to the frontier;

sentences.zh: 且漁陽之地，逼接北狄，黠虜困迫，必求其助。

translations.literal: Moreover Yuyang's territory presses against northern Di—wily barbarians hard pressed will surely seek aid.
translations.idiomatic: Yuyang borders the steppe—cornered tribes will seek barbarian allies.

sentences.zh: 又今所過縣邑，尤為困乏。

translations.literal: Moreover now counties and towns passed through are especially destitute.
translations.idiomatic: The counties along your route are desperately poor.

sentences.zh: 種麥之家，多在城郭，聞官兵將至，當已收之矣。

translations.literal: Households planting wheat—many are in outer walls—hearing official troops will arrive—presumably already harvested them.
translations.idiomatic: Farmers near the walls will have stripped their barley fields before imperial troops arrive.

sentences.zh: 大軍遠涉二千餘裏，士馬罷勞，轉糧限阻，今兗、豫、青、翼，中國之都，而寇賊從橫，未及從化。

translations.literal: Great army travels far crossing over two thousand li—soldiers and horses weary—transporting grain blocked and obstructed—now Yan, Yu, Qing, Ji—the Middle Kingdom's heart—and bandits roam unchecked—not yet submitted to transformation.
translations.idiomatic: A two-thousand-li march exhausts men and beasts while grain convoys choke — Yan, Yu, Qing, and Ji remain infested with rebels who ignore your authority.

sentences.zh: 漁陽以東，本備邊塞，地接外虜，貢稅微薄。

translations.literal: East of Yuyang originally prepared border passes—land adjoins outer barbarians—tribute and tax meager.
translations.idiomatic: East of Yuyang lies frontier country—sparse revenues from tribes beyond the wall.

sentences.zh: 安平之時，尚資內郡，況今荒耗，豈足先圖？

translations.literal: In peaceful secure times still relied on inner commanderies—how much more now wasteland exhaustion—is it enough to plan first?
translations.idiomatic: Even in peace it leaned on interior counties—now exhausted, should it be your first objective?

sentences.zh: 而陛下舍近務遠，棄易求難，四方疑怪，百姓恐懼，誠臣之所惑也。

translations.literal: Yet Your Majesty abandons near and serves far—casts aside easy and seeks hard—the four quarters doubt and marvel—the hundred clans fear and tremble—truly what your servant is perplexed by.
translations.idiomatic: Abandoning easy gains nearby for a distant gamble will unsettle the provinces and terrify the people—I cannot fathom it.

sentences.zh: 復願遠覽文王重兵博謀，近思征伐前後之宜，顧問有司，使極愚誠，采其所長，擇之聖慮，以中土為憂念。

translations.literal: Again wish broadly to survey King Wen's heavy troops and broad counsel—near reflect on expeditions' front and rear propriety—consult responsible offices—cause utmost foolish sincerity—gather what they excel at—choose in sagely deliberation—take central lands as worry and thought.
translations.idiomatic: Study King Wen's caution, weigh every phase of campaigning, consult your ministers, and put the central plains ahead of distant schemes."

sentences.zh: 』帝覽其奏，竟不親征。

translations.literal: " The emperor reviewed his memorial—in the end did not personally campaign.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor read the memorial and gave up the expedition.

sentences.zh: 時，賊徐異卿等萬餘人據富平，連攻之不下，唯雲：『願降司徒伏公。

translations.literal: At this time bandits Xu Yiqing and others over ten thousand occupied Fuping—continuously attacking did not take them down—they only said, "We wish to surrender to Minister of Education Fu.
translations.idiomatic: Bandits led by Xu Yiqing held Fuping in defiance until they said they would yield only to Grand Minister Fu Zhan.

sentences.zh: 』帝知湛為青、徐所信向，遣到平原，異卿等即日歸降，護送洛陽。

translations.literal: " The emperor knew Zhan was trusted and inclined to by Qing and Xu—sent him to Pingyuan—Yiqing and others that same day submitted—escorted to Luoyang.
translations.idiomatic: Knowing Fu commanded trust in Qing and Xu, the emperor sent him to Pingyuan; Xu surrendered the same day and was brought to Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 湛雖在倉卒，造次必於文德，以為禮樂政化之首，顛沛猶不可違。

translations.literal: Although Zhan was in haste, in sudden crises always adhered to civil virtue—taking ritual and music as head of governance and transformation—even in hardship rushing still could not violate.
translations.idiomatic: Even in crisis Fu insisted that ritual and music anchor government—principles he refused to abandon amid turmoil.

sentences.zh: 是歲奏行鄉飲酒禮，遂施行之。

translations.literal: That year he memorialized implementing village drinking ceremony—thereupon carried it out.
translations.idiomatic: That year he introduced the village drinking ceremony county-wide.

sentences.zh: 其冬，車駕徵張步，留湛居守。

translations.literal: That winter the imperial carriage campaigned against Zhang Bu—left Zhan to reside and guard.
translations.idiomatic: That winter Guangwu marched against Zhang Bu and left Fu to hold the capital.

sentences.zh: 時，蒸祭高廟，而河南尹、司隸校尉於廟中爭論，湛不舉奏，坐策免。

translations.literal: At that time steam-sacrifice at Lofty Temple—but Governor of Henan and Metropolitan Superintendent quarreled inside temple—Zhan did not memorialize impeachment—sat and was dismissed by edict.
translations.idiomatic: During the ancestral sacrifice at Gaodi the Henan governor quarreled with the metropolitan superintendent; Fu failed to report it and was stripped of office.

sentences.zh: 六年，徙封不其侯，邑三千六百戶，遣就國。

translations.literal: In the sixth year transferred enfeoffment to Marquis of Buqi—fief three thousand six hundred households—sent to proceed to state.
translations.idiomatic: In year six his title shifted to Marquis of Buqi with 3,600 households and orders to reside there.

sentences.zh: 後南陽太守杜詩上疏薦湛曰：『臣聞唐、虞以股肱康，文王以多士寧，是故《詩》稱「濟濟」，《書》曰「良哉」。

translations.literal: Later Grand Administrator of Nanyang Du Shi submitted memorial recommending Zhan, saying, "Your servant has heard Tang and Yu through thighs and arms were healthy; King Wen through many officers was secure—therefore the Odes praises 'majestic,' the Documents says 'how good.'"
translations.idiomatic: Du Shi of Nanyang later urged Fu's recall, citing how sage kings relied on worthy ministers—the Classics praise such courts.

sentences.zh: 臣詩竊見故大司徒陽都侯伏湛，自行束修，訖無毀玷，篤信好學，守死善道，經為人師，行為儀表。

translations.literal: Your servant Shi secretly sees former Grand Minister of Education Marquis of Yangdu Fu Zhan—from practicing bundles-of-gifts as youth—down to end without stain—steadfast trust fond of learning—guarding death good Way—classics made him people's teacher—conduct made model.
translations.idiomatic: Fu Zhan has been impeccable since youth—a scholar's scholar whose conduct sets the standard.

sentences.zh: 前在河內朝歌及居平原，吏人畏愛，則而象之。

translations.literal: Formerly in Henei Zhaoqi and dwelling Pingyuan—clerks and people feared and loved—patterned and modeled him.
translations.idiomatic: In Zhaoqi and Pingyuan officials both feared and loved him—and strove to imitate him.

sentences.zh: 遭時反復，不離兵兇，秉節持重，有不可奪之誌。

translations.literal: Encountering times reversed and repeated—not leaving military calamity—grasping integrity holding steady—having resolve nothing could wrest away.
translations.idiomatic: Through civil war he never abandoned principle—unshakeable resolve amid slaughter.

sentences.zh: 陛下深知其能，顯以宰相之重，眾賢百姓，仰望德義。

translations.literal: Your Majesty deeply knows his abilities—manifestly with chief minister's weight—host of worthies hundred clans—look up hoping for virtue and righteousness.
translations.idiomatic: You knew his talent and gave him the chief ministership—everyone looked to him for moral leadership.

sentences.zh: 微過斥退，久不復用，有識所惜，儒士痛心，臣竊傷之。

translations.literal: Slight fault dismissed—long not again employed—those with insight lament—Confucian scholars heart-pained—your servant secretly grieves it.
translations.idiomatic: Dismissing him for a minor lapse wastes talent—the learned mourn it bitterly.

sentences.zh: 湛容貌堂堂，國之光輝；

translations.literal: Zhan's bearing appearance august—the state's glorious radiance;
translations.idiomatic: Fu Zhan's commanding presence brings honor to the state;

sentences.zh: 智略謀慮，朝之淵藪。

translations.literal: wisdom stratagem planning thought—the court's deep pool.
translations.idiomatic: his counsel is the court's deepest reservoir of wisdom.

sentences.zh: 髫發厲誌，白首不衰。

translations.literal: Topknot hair sharpened resolve—white head not declining.
translations.idiomatic: From boyhood zeal to white hair his vigor never flagged.

sentences.zh: 實足以先後王室，名足以光示遠人。

translations.literal: Truly sufficient to order front and rear royal house—name sufficient to brightly display distant people.
translations.idiomatic: He can still anchor the royal house and lend prestige abroad.

sentences.zh: 古者選擢諸侯以為公卿，是故四方回首，仰望京師。

translations.literal: Anciently selecting raising feudal lords to make dukes and ministers—therefore four quarters turn heads—look up hoping capital.
translations.idiomatic: Ancient kings promoted regional lords to high office—making the realm look to the capital again.

sentences.zh: 柱石之臣，宜居輔弼，出入禁門，補缺拾遺。

translations.literal: Pillar and stone ministers—fittingly should dwell assisting—coming going forbidden gates—mending gaps gathering oversights.
translations.idiomatic: Such pillar ministers belong beside the throne—passing the palace gates to remedy every oversight.

sentences.zh: 臣詩愚戇，不足以知宰相之才，竊懷區區，敢不自竭。

translations.literal: Your servant Shi is foolish blunt—not sufficient to know chief minister's talents—secretly harbors petty sincerity—dares not fail to exhaust self.
translations.idiomatic: I am too blunt to judge chief ministers—but beg you with all sincerity.

sentences.zh: 臣前為侍御史，上封事，言湛公廉愛下，好惡分明，累世儒學，素持名信，經明行修，通達國政，尤宜近侍，納言左右，舊制九州五尚書，令一郡二人，可以湛代。

translations.literal: Your servant formerly was Palace Assistant Clerk—submitted sealed memorial—stating Zhan fair incorrupt loving inferiors—good evil distinct—accumulated generations Confucian learning—always holding fame trust—classics clear conduct cultivated—penetrating reaching state governance—especially fitting close attendance—speaking intake left right—old system nine provinces five Masters of Writing—order one commandery two persons—may use Zhan replace.
translations.idiomatic: As palace clerk I once memorialized that Fu is honest, loves his people, and masters policy—ideal for the Masters of Writing—under the old nine-province scheme two men per circuit could serve; Fu should fill one seat.

sentences.zh: 頗為執事所非。

translations.literal: Rather was opposed by those handling affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Those in power dismissed the idea.

sentences.zh: 但臣詩蒙恩深渥，所言誠有益於國，雖死無恨，故復越職觸冒以聞。』

translations.literal: Only your servant Shi receives grace deep and rich—what is spoken truly benefits the state—although death without regret—therefore again overstepping duty risking offense to report."
translations.idiomatic: Yet graced by deep favor I speak though it costs my life—again overstepping rank to urge this."

sentences.zh: 十三年夏，徵，敕尚書擇拜吏日，未及就位，因宴見中暑，病卒。

translations.literal: Summer thirteenth year—summoned—ordered Masters of Writing choose appointment clerk day—before reaching assumption of post—owing to feast audience suffered summer heat—illness died.
translations.idiomatic: Summoned in summer of year thirteen, he collapsed from heatstroke at a banquet before taking office and died.

sentences.zh: 賜秘器，帝親吊祠，遣使者送喪修冢。

translations.literal: Granted secret burial vessels—emperor personally condoled sacrifice—dispatched envoys send funeral repair tomb.
translations.idiomatic: The court supplied a noble coffin; the emperor mourned in person and sent envoys to bury him with honor.

sentences.zh: 二子：隆、翕。

translations.literal: Two sons: Long and Xi.
translations.idiomatic: He left two sons: Fu Long and Fu Xi.

sentences.zh: 初，自伏生已後，世傳經學，清靜無競，故東州號為『伏不鬥』雲。

translations.literal: Formerly, from Fu Sheng onward—generations transmitted classical learning—pure quiet without contention—therefore eastern provinces titled them "Fu does not fight," it is said.
translations.idiomatic: Since Old Master Fu's day the clan taught classics in quiet humility—folk in the east called them "the Fus who never quarrel."

sentences.zh: 隆字伯文，少以節操立名，仕郡督郵。

translations.literal: Long courtesy name Bowen—young by integrity conduct established name—served commandery as inspector.
translations.idiomatic: Fu Long, styled Bowen, earned renown for integrity as a commandery inspector.

sentences.zh: 建武二年，詣懷宮，光武甚親接之。

translations.literal: In second year of Jianwu—went to Huai palace—Guangwu very intimately received him.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 2 he reported to Guangwu's Huai palace camp and won warm favor.

sentences.zh: 時，張步兄弟各擁強兵，據有齊地，拜隆為太中大夫，持節使青、徐二州，招降郡國。

translations.literal: At this time Zhang Bu brothers each grasped strong troops—occupied Qi territory—appointed Long Grand Counselor of the Palace—holding tally envoy to Qing and Xu two provinces—summon surrender commanderies and kingdoms.
translations.idiomatic: While Zhang Bu held Qi the court named Fu Long Grand Counselor with imperial tally to win over Qing and Xu.

sentences.zh: 隆移檄告曰：『乃者，猾臣王莽，殺帝盜位。

translations.literal: Long moved proclamation declaring, saying, "Recently—crafty minister Wang Mang—killed emperor stole position.
translations.idiomatic: His proclamation read: Wang Mang murdered an emperor and seized the throne.

sentences.zh: 宗室興兵，除亂誅莽，故群下推立聖公，以主宗廟。

translations.literal: Imperial lineage raised troops—removed chaos executed Mang—therefore host below pushed establishing Holy Duke—to master ancestral temple.
translations.idiomatic: The Liu clan destroyed Mang and raised Emperor Gengshi to restore the temples.

sentences.zh: 而任用賊臣，殺戮賢良，三王作亂，盜賊從橫，忤逆天心，卒為赤眉所害。

translations.literal: Yet employed villain ministers—slaughtered worthy good—three kings made rebellion—bandits roamed unchecked—contravened Heaven's heart—finally was harmed by Red Eyebrows.
translations.idiomatic: Yet he trusted villains, slew worthies, unleashed revolt and banditry, off Heaven—and fell to the Red Eyebrows.

sentences.zh: 皇天祐漢，聖哲應期，陛下神武奮發，以少制眾。

translations.literal: August Heaven aids Han—sage wise responds period—Your Majesty divine martial rouses forth—with few controlling many.
translations.idiomatic: Heaven preserves Han—Your Majesty arose in martial brilliance and defeats vast hosts with handfuls of men.

sentences.zh: 故尋、邑以百萬之軍，潰散於昆陽，王郎以全趙之師，土崩於邯鄲，大肜、高胡望旗消靡，鐵脛、五校莫不摧破。

translations.literal: Therefore Xun and Yi with million troops—scattered dissolved at Kunyang—Wang Lang with whole Zhao army—earth collapsed at Handan—Big Forehead Gao Hu—seeing flags melted away—Iron Shin Five Camps none not smashed broken.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Xun's horde shattered at Kunyang, Wang Lang collapsed at Handan, and every named band—Iron Shin, Five Camps—crumbled before your banners.

sentences.zh: 梁王劉永，幸以宗室屬籍，爵為侯王，不知厭足，自求禍棄，遂封爵牧守，造為詐逆。

translations.literal: Liang king Liu Yong—fortunately by imperial lineage register—nobility became marquis king—not knowing satisfaction—self sought calamity discard—thereupon enfeoffed noble shepherds guards—made deceitful rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: Liu Yong of Liang abused imperial kinship for kingship—never content—and carved up offices in treason.

sentences.zh: 今虎牙大將軍屯營十萬，已拔睢陽，劉永奔迸，家已族矣。

translations.literal: Now Tiger-Fang Grand General camps hundred thousand—already captured Suiyang—Liu Yong fled scattered—family already exterminated.
translations.idiomatic: The Tiger-Fang general's hundred thousand men have taken Suiyang—Yong flees and his clan is destroyed.

sentences.zh: 此諸君所聞也。

translations.literal: This is what you gentlemen have heard.
translations.idiomatic: You have all heard this.

sentences.zh: 不先自圖。

translations.literal: Not first plan for yourselves.
translations.idiomatic: Act while you can.

sentences.zh: 後悔何及！

translations.literal: After regret how reach!
translations.idiomatic: Regret later will be too late!

sentences.zh: 』青、徐群盜得此惶怖，獲索賊右師郎等六校即時皆降。

translations.literal: " Qing and Xu bandit hosts obtaining this fearful terrified—Huo-Suo bandit Right Instructor Lang and six camps instantly all surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: " Qing and Xu brigands panicked—six camps including Right Instructor Lang of the Huo-Suo band surrendered at once.

sentences.zh: 張步遣使隨隆，詣闕上書，獻鰒魚。

translations.literal: Zhang Bu dispatched envoy following Long—to palace gate submitting memorial—presented abalone fish.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Bu sent envoys with Fu Long to court with abalone as tribute.

sentences.zh: 其冬，拜隆光祿大夫，復使於步，並與新除青州牧守及都尉俱東，詔隆輒拜令長以下。

translations.literal: That winter appointed Long Counselor of the Palace—again envoy to Bu—and together with newly appointed Qing province shepherd guards and commandant all east—ordered Long immediately appoint magistrates chiefs below.
translations.idiomatic: That winter Fu Long became Counselor of the Palace and returned east with new Qing officials—empowered to appoint county magistrates.

sentences.zh: 隆招懷綏緝，多來降附。

translations.literal: Long summoned cherished soothed gathered—many came surrendering attaching.
translations.idiomatic: His conciliatory work drew many submissions.

sentences.zh: 帝嘉其功，比之酈生。

translations.literal: The emperor praised his merit—compared him to Li Sheng.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor likened him to Li Yiji the persuader.

sentences.zh: 即拜步為東萊太守，而劉永復遣使立步為齊王。

translations.literal: Immediately appointed Bu as grand administrator of Donglai—but Liu Yong again dispatched envoy establishing Bu as Qi king.
translations.idiomatic: The court named Zhang Bu grand administrator of Donglai while Yong tried to crown him king of Qi.

sentences.zh: 步貪受王爵，豫未決。

translations.literal: Bu greedy accepted king nobility—hesitation not yet decided.
translations.idiomatic: Tempted by Yong's kingship, Zhang hesitated.

sentences.zh: 隆曉譬曰：『高祖與天下約，非劉氏不王，今可得為十萬戶侯耳。

translations.literal: Long dawned analogy saying, "High Ancestor covenanted with realm—not Liu surname not king—now can obtain being hundred-thousand-household marquis only.
translations.idiomatic: Fu warned him: Gaozu swore only Lius could be kings—you may earn a great marquisate at best.

sentences.zh: 』步欲留隆與共守二州，隆不聽，求得反命，步遂執隆而受永封。

translations.literal: " Bu wished to detain Long together guarding two provinces—Long did not listen—sought obtaining return report—Bu thereupon seized Long yet accepted Yong's enfeoffment.
translations.idiomatic: " Zhang asked Fu to stay; Fu refused and sought leave to report—Zhang then held him hostage while accepting Yong's title.

sentences.zh: 隆遣間使上書曰：『臣隆奉使無狀，受執兇逆，雖在困厄，授命不顧。

translations.literal: Long dispatched secret envoy submitting memorial saying, "Your servant Long carried mission without form—seized by vicious rebel—although in hardship distress—offering life without regard.
translations.idiomatic: Fu smuggled a memorial: his mission failed and rebels held him—yet he would die before yielding.

sentences.zh: 又吏人知步反畔，心不附之，願以時進兵，無以臣隆為念。

translations.literal: Moreover clerks and people know Bu rebelled defied—hearts do not attach to him—wish timely advance troops—do not take your servant Long as thought.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang's men know his treason—strike now and do not spare me.

sentences.zh: 臣隆得生到闕廷，受誅有司，此其大願；

translations.literal: Your servant Long can live arrive palace court—receive execution responsible offices—this is great wish;
translations.idiomatic: If I reach court for execution I will have my wish;

sentences.zh: 若令沒身寇手，以父母昆弟長累陛下。

translations.literal: if order sink body bandit hands—with parents brothers elder younger long burden Your Majesty.
translations.idiomatic: if I die in captivity my kin become your burden.

sentences.zh: 陛下與皇後、太子永享萬國，與天無極。

translations.literal: Your Majesty with empress crown prince eternally enjoy myriad lands—with Heaven without limit.
translations.idiomatic: May you, the empress, and the heir enjoy endless reign.

sentences.zh: 』帝得隆奏，召父湛流涕以示之曰：『隆可謂有蘇武之節。

translations.literal: " The emperor obtained Long's memorial—summoned father Zhan weeping tears to show him saying, "Long may be called having Su Wu's integrity.
translations.idiomatic: Reading it Guangwu summoned Fu Zhan in tears: "Your son has Su Wu's steadfastness."

sentences.zh: 恨不且許而遽求還也！

translations.literal: Regret not temporarily granting yet hastily seeking return!
translations.idiomatic: I regret we granted him leave to return so soon!

sentences.zh: 』其後步遂殺之，時人莫不憐哀焉。

translations.literal: " Afterward Bu then killed him—people of the time none did not pity mourn him.
translations.idiomatic: " Zhang Bu later executed him—the realm mourned.

sentences.zh: 五年，張步平，車駕幸北海，詔隆中弟鹹收隆喪，賜給棺斂，太中大夫護送喪事，詔告瑯邪作冢，以子瑗為郎中。

translations.literal: Fifth year—Zhang Bu pacified—imperial carriage favored Beihai—ordered Long middle younger brother Xian collect Long funeral—granted supplied coffin burial—Grand Counselor of Palace escorted funeral affairs—ordered instruct Langya make tomb—appointed son Yuan Gentleman.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhang fell in year five Guangwu visited Beihai and ordered Fu Xian to recover Fu Long's body—provided a state funeral escort and burial in Langya—and named Long's son Yuan a Gentleman.

sentences.zh: 侯霸字君房，河南密人也。

translations.literal: Hou Ba, courtesy name Junfang, was a man of Mi in Henan.
translations.idiomatic: Hou Ba, styled Junfang, came from Mi in Henan.

sentences.zh: 族父淵，以宦者有才辯，任職元帝時，佐右顯等領中書，號曰大常侍。

translations.literal: Clan uncle Yuan—because eunuchs had talent debate—served office Emperor Yuan's time—assisted Shi Xian etc. leading palace secretariat—titled Grand Regular Attendant.
translations.idiomatic: His kinsman Hou Yuan was a talented eunuch who served Emperor Yuan under Shi Xian as a senior palace secretary.

sentences.zh: 成帝時，任霸為太子舍人。

translations.literal: In Emperor Cheng's time appointed Ba as heir-apparent householderman.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Cheng Hou Ba became a gentleman attendant at the heir's palace.

sentences.zh: 霸矜嚴有威容，家累千金，不事產業。

translations.literal: Ba stern strict had awesome bearing—family accumulated thousand gold—not engaging productive estates.
translations.idiomatic: Grave and imposing, from a wealthy family, he ignored business pursuits.

sentences.zh: 篤誌好學，師事九江太守房元，治《穀梁春秋》，為元都講。

translations.literal: Steadfast resolve fond of learning—served teacher Jiujiang grand administrator Fang Yuan—studied Guliang Spring and Autumn—became Yuan's chief lecturer.
translations.idiomatic: Devoted to study under Fang Yuan of Jiujiang he mastered the Guliang commentary and became Fang's chief lecturer.

sentences.zh: 王莽初，王威司命陳崇舉霸德行，遷隨宰。

translations.literal: Wang Mang's beginning—Wang Wei Commander of Evaluation Chen Chong recommended Ba's conduct virtue—promoted Sui magistrate.
translations.idiomatic: Early in Wang Mang's reign Chen Chong recommended Hou's character and he became magistrate of Sui.

sentences.zh: 縣界曠遠，濱帶江湖，而亡命者多為寇盜。

translations.literal: County borders vast distant—margin belts rivers lakes—but fugitives many became bandit robbers.
translations.idiomatic: The county was vast and river-bound—fugitives turned bandit.

sentences.zh: 霸到，即案誅豪猾，分捕山賊，縣中清靜。

translations.literal: When Ba arrived—immediately investigated executed powerful crafty—divided capture mountain bandits—county within pure quiet.
translations.idiomatic: Hou executed powerful criminals and swept the hills until order returned.

sentences.zh: 再遷為執法刺奸，糾案勢位者，無所疑憚。

translations.literal: Again promoted to law-enforcing stab wicked—investigate powerful position holders—nothing doubting fearing.
translations.idiomatic: Promoted to inspector he prosecuted the powerful without flinching.

sentences.zh: 後為淮平大尹，政理有能名。

translations.literal: Later became Huai grand governor—governance administration had capable name.
translations.idiomatic: As Huai-ping governor he earned renown for competence.

sentences.zh: 及王莽之敗，霸保固自守，卒全一郡。

translations.literal: When Wang Mang fell, Ba guarded fastness and held himself secure—in the end preserving whole commandery.
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Mang collapsed Hou Ba held Huai-ping firm and saved the entire commandery.

sentences.zh: 更始元年，遣使徵霸，百姓老弱相攜號哭，遮使者車，或當道而臥。

translations.literal: Gengshi first year—dispatched envoy summoning Ba—hundred clans old weak mutually led wailing—blocked envoy's carriage—or lay across road.
translations.idiomatic: In Gengshi 1 an envoy came to recall Hou; young and old wept, blocked the carriage, and lay in the road.

sentences.zh: 皆曰：『願乞侯君復留期年。

translations.literal: All said, "We beg Lord Marquis again remain one full year.
translations.idiomatic: They begged Marquis Hou to stay another year.

sentences.zh: 』民至乃戒乳婦勿得舉子，侯君當去，必不能合。

translations.literal: " People went so far as to warn nursing women not to bear children—Lord Marquis about to depart—surely cannot unite.
translations.idiomatic: Women were urged not to bear children while Hou might leave—such was their despair.

sentences.zh: 使者慮霸就徵，臨淮必亂，不敢授璽書，具以狀聞。

translations.literal: Envoy feared Ba answering summons—Linhuai surely chaotic—did not dare confer sealed edict—fully reported situation upward.
translations.idiomatic: Fearing riot if Hou left, the envoy refused the edict and reported the truth.

sentences.zh: 會更始敗，道路不通。

translations.literal: It happened Gengshi fell—roads not passable.
translations.idiomatic: Then Gengshi fell and roads closed.

sentences.zh: 建武四年，光武徵霸與車駕會壽春，拜尚書令。

translations.literal: Jianwu fourth year—Guangwu summoned Ba to meet imperial carriage at Shouchun—appointed Prefect of Masters of Writing.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 4 Guangwu summoned Hou Ba to Shouchun and named him Prefect of Masters of Writing.

sentences.zh: 時無故典，朝廷又少舊臣，霸明習故事，收錄遺文，條奏前世善政法度有益於時者，皆施行之。

translations.literal: At that time no former precedents—court moreover few old ministers—Ba clearly practiced old affairs—gathered recorded stray texts—itemized memorialized former generations good governance laws beneficial to age—all implemented them.
translations.idiomatic: With precedents lost and elder ministers scarce, Hou recovered old statutes and memorialized workable policies from earlier reigns.

sentences.zh: 每春下寬大之詔，奉四時之令，皆霸所建也。

translations.literal: Every spring issued lenient edicts—upholding four seasons' ordinances—all were what Ba established.
translations.idiomatic: The spring amnesties and seasonal edicts all stemmed from his drafts.

sentences.zh: 明年，代伏湛為大司徒，封關內侯。

translations.literal: Next year—replaced Fu Zhan as Grand Minister of Education—enfeoffed Marquis Within the Passes.
translations.idiomatic: The next year he succeeded Fu Zhan as Grand Minister of Education with a marquisate inside the passes.

sentences.zh: 在位明察守正，奉公不回。

translations.literal: In office bright insight guarding rectitude—serving public not turning.
translations.idiomatic: Scrupulous and upright in office, he never bent private interest.

sentences.zh: 十三年，霸薨，帝深傷惜之，親自臨吊。

translations.literal: Thirteenth year—Ba died—emperor deeply grieved—personally attended condolence.
translations.idiomatic: In year thirteen Hou Ba died; Guangwu mourned him personally.

sentences.zh: 下詔曰：『惟霸積善清潔。

translations.literal: Issued edict saying, "Only Ba accumulated goodness pure clean.
translations.idiomatic: An edict ran: Hou Ba lived in unstained virtue.

sentences.zh: 視事九年。

translations.literal: Presided over affairs nine years.
translations.idiomatic: He served nine years in office.

sentences.zh: 漢家舊制，丞相拜曰，封為列侯。

translations.literal: Han house old system—chancellor appointment day—enfeoffed as ranked marquis.
translations.idiomatic: Han custom made chief ministers full marquises on appointment.

sentences.zh: 朕以軍師暴露，功臣未封，緣忠臣之義，不欲相逾，未及爵命，奄然而終。

translations.literal: We because army hosts exposed to elements—meritorious ministers not yet enfeoffed—following loyal ministers' righteousness—not wishing mutually to exceed—before reaching noble orders—suddenly ended.
translations.idiomatic: While armies campaigned in the field I deferred Hou's full enfeoffment to avoid slighting other loyalists—before the patent arrived he was gone.

sentences.zh: 嗚呼哀哉！

translations.literal: Alas how sorrowful!
translations.idiomatic: Alas for him!

sentences.zh: 』於是追封謚霸則鄉哀侯，食邑二千六百戶。

translations.literal: " Thereupon posthumously enfeoffed posthumous title Ba Ze Township Grieving Marquis—fief two thousand six hundred households.
translations.idiomatic: The court posthumously named him Marquis Ai of Zexiang with 2,600 households.

sentences.zh: 子昱嗣。

translations.literal: Son Yu succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: His son Hou Yu inherited the title.

sentences.zh: 臨淮吏人共為立祠，四時祭焉。

translations.literal: Linhai clerks and people together for him erected shrine—four seasons sacrifice there.
translations.idiomatic: Linhai built him a shrine with seasonal offerings.

sentences.zh: 以沛郡太守韓歆代霸為大司徒。

translations.literal: With Pei commandery grand administrator Han Xin replacing Ba as Grand Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: Han Xin of Pei succeeded him as Grand Minister of Education.

sentences.zh: 歆字翁君，南陽人，以從攻伐有功，封扶陽侯。

translations.literal: Xin courtesy name Wengjun—Nanyang man—by following campaigns attacking subduing had merit—enfeoffed Marquis of Fuyang.
translations.idiomatic: Han Xin, styled Wengjun, from Nanyang, earned the title Marquis of Fuyang for campaigning with Guangwu.

sentences.zh: 好直言，無隱諱，帝每不能容。

translations.literal: Fond of straight speech—no concealed taboo—emperor often could not endure.
translations.idiomatic: Blunt to a fault, he often tried Guangwu's patience.

sentences.zh: 嘗因朝會，聞帝讀隗囂、公孫述相與書，歆曰：『亡國之君皆有才，桀、紂亦有才。

translations.literal: Once owing to court assembly—heard emperor read Wei Xiao Gongsun Shu mutual letters—Xin said, "Doomed state's rulers all have talent—Jie Zhou also have talent.
translations.idiomatic: Once when the emperor read correspondence between Wei Xiao and Gongsun Shu, Han said fallen rulers can still be clever—Jie and Zhou had talent too.

sentences.zh: 』帝大怒，以為激發。

translations.literal: " Emperor greatly angry—thought provoked incitement.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu took it as a deliberate insult.

sentences.zh: 歆又證歲將饑兇，指天畫地，言甚剛切，坐免歸田裏。

translations.literal: Xin again testified year about famine vicious—pointing Heaven tracing earth—speech very blunt cutting—sat dismissed returned hamlet fields.
translations.idiomatic: He next warned of famine with wild gestures—too blunt—and was dismissed to his estate.

sentences.zh: 帝猶不釋，復遣使宣詔責之。

translations.literal: Emperor still not released—again dispatched envoy proclaim edict blame him.
translations.idiomatic: Still unsatisfied, Guangwu sent edicts denouncing him.

sentences.zh: 司隸校尉鮑永固請不能得，歆及子嬰竟自殺。

translations.literal: Metropolitan Superintendent Bao Yong firmly requested could not obtain—Xin and son Ying finally committed suicide.
translations.idiomatic: Despite Bao Yong's pleas Han Xin and his son Han Ying killed themselves.

sentences.zh: 歆素有重名，死非其罪，眾多不厭，帝乃追賜錢穀，以成禮葬之。

translations.literal: Xin always had heavy name—death not his crime—multitude many unsatisfied—emperor then posthumously granted money grain—to complete ritual bury him.
translations.idiomatic: Han had been respected—his death seemed unjust—so the court funded a proper funeral.

sentences.zh: 昱後徒封於陵侯，永平中兼太僕。

translations.literal: Yu later transferred enfeoffment Marquis of Yuling—Yongping middle concurrently Grand Coachman.
translations.idiomatic: Hou Yu later became Marquis of Yuling and served as Grand Coachman under Yongping.

sentences.zh: 昱卒，子建嗣。

translations.literal: Yu died—son Jian succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: Yu died and Jian inherited.

sentences.zh: 建卒，子昌嗣。

translations.literal: Jian died—son Chang succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: Jian was succeeded by Chang.

sentences.zh: 宋弘字仲子，京兆長安人也。

translations.literal: Song Hong courtesy name Zhongzi—was man of Chang'an in Jingzhao.
translations.idiomatic: Song Hong, styled Zhongzi, came from Chang'an in Jingzhao.

sentences.zh: 父尚，成帝時至少府。

translations.literal: Father Shang—in Emperor Cheng's time reached Privy Treasurer.
translations.idiomatic: His father Song Shang rose to Privy Treasurer under Emperor Cheng.

sentences.zh: 哀帝立，以不附董賢，違忤抵罪。

translations.literal: Emperor Ai established—because not attaching Dong Xian—contravened offended incurred guilt.
translations.idiomatic: Under Emperor Ai he refused to flatter Dong Xian and paid with prosecution.

sentences.zh: 弘少而溫順，哀、平間作侍中，王莽時為共工。

translations.literal: Hong young was mild compliant—Ai Ping between served Palace Attendant—Wang Mang time was Minister of Works.
translations.idiomatic: Song grew up gentle, served as Palace Attendant under Ai and Ping, then as Minister of Public Works under Wang Mang.

sentences.zh: 赤眉入長安，遣使徵弘，逼迫不得已，行至渭橋，自投於水，家人救得出，因佯死獲免。

translations.literal: Red Eyebrows entered Chang'an—dispatched envoy summoning Hong—pressed compelled without choice—walked reached Wei Bridge—self threw into water—family members rescued obtained exit—thereupon feigned death obtained exemption.
translations.idiomatic: When the Red Eyebrows summoned him he threw himself into the Wei River, was rescued, then feigned death to escape.

sentences.zh: 光武即位，徵拜太中大夫。

translations.literal: Guangwu ascended throne—summoned appointed Grand Counselor of the Palace.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu appointed him Grand Counselor of the Palace.

sentences.zh: 建武二年，代王梁為大司空，封栒邑侯。

translations.literal: Jianwu second year—replaced Wang Liang as Minister of Works—enfeoffed Marquis of Xunyi.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianwu 2 he succeeded Wang Liang as Minister of Works with the title Marquis of Xunyi.

sentences.zh: 所得租奉分贍九族，家無資產，以清行致稱。

translations.literal: Obtained rent salary divided supplied nine clans—household without assets—by pure conduct achieved praise.
translations.idiomatic: He shared every stipend with kin and kept no savings—praised for integrity.

sentences.zh: 徙封宣平侯。

translations.literal: Transferred enfeoffment Marquis of Xuanping.
translations.idiomatic: His title moved to Marquis of Xuanping.

sentences.zh: 帝嘗問弘通博之士，弘乃薦沛國醒譚才學洽聞，幾能及楊雄、劉向父子。

translations.literal: Emperor once asked Hong broadly learned scholars—Hong then recommended Pei kingdom Huan Tan talent learning comprehensive hearing—almost able reach Yang Xiong Liu Xiang father son.
translations.idiomatic: When asked for learned men Song recommended Huan Tan of Pei—a scholar whose breadth rivaled Yang Xiong and Liu Xiang.

sentences.zh: 於是召譚拜議郎、給事中。

translations.literal: Thereupon summoned Tan appointed Consultant Attendant at the Palace.
translations.idiomatic: Huan Tan was summoned as Consultant and palace attendant.

sentences.zh: 帝每宴，輒令鼓琴，好其繁聲。

translations.literal: Emperor every feast—always ordered strum zither—liked its intricate sounds.
translations.idiomatic: At banquets the emperor made him play lavish zither music.

sentences.zh: 弘聞之不悅，悔於薦舉，伺譚內出，正朝服坐府上，遣吏召之。

translations.literal: Hong hearing it not pleased—regretted recommendation—waited Tan inner exit—straightened court robes sat atop office—sent clerk summon him.
translations.idiomatic: Song regretted the recommendation; when Huan left the palace Song sat in full court dress and summoned him.

sentences.zh: 譚至，不與席而讓之曰：『吾所以薦子者，欲令輔國家以道德也，而今數進鄭聲以亂《雅》、《頌》，非忠正者也。

translations.literal: Tan arrived—not gave mat but blamed him saying, "What I recommended you for was wishing to order aid state with virtue and Way—yet now repeatedly advancing Zheng sounds to disorder Odes and Eulogia—not loyal upright one.
translations.idiomatic: Song refused him a seat: "I meant you to guide the state with virtue—instead you feed the ruler decadent tunes that corrupt the classics."

sentences.zh: 能自改邪？

translations.literal: Able self reform?
translations.idiomatic: Will you mend your ways?

sentences.zh: 將令相舉以法乎？

translations.literal: Or shall order mutually impeach by law?
translations.idiomatic: Or shall I impeach you myself?

sentences.zh: 』譚頓首辭謝，良久乃遣之。

translations.literal: " Tan knocked head declined apologized—long time then dismissed him.
translations.idiomatic: Huan kowtowed in apology before Song dismissed him.

sentences.zh: 後大會群臣，帝使譚鼓琴，譚見弘，失其常度。

translations.literal: Later great assembly host of ministers—emperor ordered Tan strum zither—Tan saw Hong—lost his constant measure.
translations.idiomatic: Later at a court feast when ordered to play, Huan faltered at sight of Song.

sentences.zh: 帝怪而問之。

translations.literal: Emperor wondered and asked him.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor asked why.

sentences.zh: 弘乃離席免冠謝曰：『臣所以薦醒譚者，望能以忠正導主，而令朝廷耽悅鄭聲，臣之罪也。

translations.literal: Hong then left mat removed cap apologized saying, "Your servant what recommended Xing Tan for—hoped able by loyalty upright guide lord—yet caused court addicted to Zheng sounds—your servant's crime.
translations.idiomatic: Song left his seat bareheaded: "I recommended Huan Tan to guide you with integrity—instead the court wallows in vulgar music—that is my fault."

sentences.zh: 』帝改容謝，使反服，其後遂不復令譚給事中。

translations.literal: " Emperor changed expression apologized—ordered return garments—afterward then no longer ordered Tan attendant at palace.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor apologized and restored Song's cap; Huan never served in the palace again.

sentences.zh: 弘推進賢士馮翊桓梁三十餘人，或相及為公卿者。

translations.literal: Hong pushed forward worthy scholars Fengyi Huan Liang thirty-odd men—or mutually reached becoming dukes ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Song promoted more than thirty men including Huan Liang of Fengyi—several reached ministerial rank.
