sentences.zh: 楊素有譏議，帝常嫌之，既見霸奏，疑其有奸，大怒，賜霸璽書曰：『崇山、幽都何可偶，黃鉞一下無處所。

translations.literal: Yang always had ridicule criticism—emperor constantly disliked him—already seeing Ba memorial—suspected had villainy—greatly angry—granted Ba sealed edict saying, "Lofty Mountain Dark Capital how can pair—yellow axe one fall no place.
translations.idiomatic: Guangwu distrusted Yan; seeing Hou's memorial he raged and sent a sealed threat of exile and execution.

sentences.zh: 欲以身試法邪？

translations.literal: Wish use body test law?
translations.idiomatic: Do you seek death?

sentences.zh: 將殺身以成仁邪？

translations.literal: Or kill body to complete benevolence?
translations.idiomatic: Or martyrdom?

sentences.zh: 』使勤奉策至司徒府。

translations.literal: " Ordered Qin carry edict arrive Minister of Education office.
translations.idiomatic: He sent Feng Qin with the edict to Hou Ba.

sentences.zh: 勤還，陳霸本意，申釋事理，帝意稍解，拜勤尚書仆射。

translations.literal: Qin returned—stated Ba's original intent—explained released matters—emperor's intent slightly relaxed—appointed Qin Masters of Writing vice director.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Qin explained Hou Ba's intent and mollified Guangwu, who named him vice director of the Masters of Writing.

sentences.zh: 職事十五年，以勤勞賜爵關內侯。

translations.literal: Office duties fifteen years—by diligent toil granted noble Marquis Within the Passes.
translations.idiomatic: After fifteen years of service he received a Marquis Within the Passes for diligence.

sentences.zh: 遷尚書令，拜大司農，三歲遷司徒。

translations.literal: Promoted Prefect of Masters of Writing—appointed Minister of Agriculture—three years promoted Minister of Education.
translations.idiomatic: He rose to Prefect of Masters of Writing, then Minister of Agriculture, then Minister of Education within three years.

sentences.zh: 先是，三公多見罪退，帝賢勤，欲令以善自終，乃因宴見從容戒之曰：『朱浮上不忠於君，下陵轢同列，竟以中傷至今，死生吉兇未可知，豈不惜哉！

translations.literal: Before this—three dukes many seen guilt dismissed—emperor worthy Qin—wished order by good self finish—thereby owing feast audience leisurely warned saying, "Zhu Fu above not loyal to lord—below rode roughshod same ranks—finally by slander harm until now—death life auspicious ominous not yet know—how not pity!
translations.idiomatic: Many dukes had fallen; Guangwu admired Feng Qin and warned him at leisure: Zhu Fu betrayed superiors and peers—ruined by slander—what a waste!

sentences.zh: 人臣放逐受誅，雖復追加賞賜賻祭，不足以償不訾之身。

translations.literal: Ministers banished executed receiving punishment—although again add posthumous gifts condolence sacrifice—not sufficient repay priceless body.
translations.idiomatic: Posthumous honors cannot repay a ruined life.

sentences.zh: 忠臣孝子，覽照前世，以為鏡誡。

translations.literal: Loyal ministers filial sons—view illuminate former generations—take as mirror warning.
translations.idiomatic: Let loyal sons study history as their mirror.

sentences.zh: 能盡忠於國，事君無二，則爵賞光乎當世，功名列於不朽，可不勉哉！

translations.literal: Able exhaust loyalty to state—serve lord without two—then noble rewards shine in present age—merit name listed in imperishable—can not strive!
translations.idiomatic: Serve one lord faithfully—win honors in life and renown beyond death—strive!

sentences.zh: 』勤愈恭約盡忠，號稱任職。

translations.literal: " Qin increasingly respectful restrained exhausted loyalty—titled called holding office.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Qin grew humbler and earned renown for competence.

sentences.zh: 勤母年八十，每會見，詔敕勿拜，令御者扶上殿，顧謂諸王主曰：『使勤貴寵者，此母也。

translations.literal: Qin's mother age eighty—every meeting audience—ordered instructed not bow—ordered charioteer support ascend hall—looked told various kings princesses saying, "That which makes Qin noble favored—this mother.
translations.idiomatic: At eighty his mother need not bow—Guangwu had her helped to the hall and told the royals she made Feng Qin's career.

sentences.zh: 』其見親重如此。

translations.literal: " Such was his seen intimate weight.
translations.idiomatic: Such was imperial favor toward him.

sentences.zh: 中元元年，薨，帝悼惜之，使者吊祠，賜東園秘器，赗贈有加。

translations.literal: Zhongyuan first year—died—emperor mourned pitied—envoys condolence sacrifice—granted eastern park secret vessels—condolence gifts increased.
translations.idiomatic: He died in Zhongyuan 1; envoys mourned him with noble coffin and rich funeral gifts.

sentences.zh: 勤七子。

translations.literal: Qin seven sons.
translations.idiomatic: Feng Qin had seven sons.

sentences.zh: 長子宗嗣，至張掖屬國都尉。

translations.literal: Eldest son Zong succeeded—reached Zhangye dependent state commandant.
translations.idiomatic: Eldest son Feng Zong inherited and rose to commandant of Zhangye dependent state.

sentences.zh: 中子順，尚平陽長公主，終於大鴻臚。

translations.literal: Middle son Shun—married Pingyang elder princess—ended at Grand Herald.
translations.idiomatic: Middle son Feng Shun married Princess Pingyang and died as Grand Herald.

sentences.zh: 建初八年，以順中子奮襲主爵為平陽侯，薨，無子。

translations.literal: Jianchu eighth year—by Shun middle son Fen inheriting mistress noble became Pingyang marquis—died—without son.
translations.idiomatic: In Jianchu 8 Fen inherited Pingyang but died heirless.

sentences.zh: 永元七年，詔書復封奮兄羽林右監勁為平陽侯，奉公主之祀。

translations.literal: Yongyuan seventh year—edict again enfeoffed Fen elder brother Right Supervisor of Household Guards Jin as Pingyang marquis—serve princess sacrifice.
translations.idiomatic: In Yongyuan 7 Jin succeeded to maintain the princess's sacrifices.

sentences.zh: 奮弟由，黃門侍郎，尚平安公主。

translations.literal: Fen's younger brother You—Yellow Gates attendant—married Ping'an princess.
translations.idiomatic: Brother Feng You served at the Yellow Gates and married Princess Ping'an.

sentences.zh: 勁薨，子卯嗣。

translations.literal: Jin died—son Mao succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: Jin died and Mao succeeded.

sentences.zh: 卯延光中為侍中，薨，子留嗣。

translations.literal: Mao Yanlong middle became Palace Attendant—died—son Liu succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: Mao served as Palace Attendant under Yanlong and was succeeded by Liu.

sentences.zh: 趙憙字伯陽，南陽宛人也。

translations.literal: Zhao Xi courtesy name Boyang—was man of Wan in Nanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Xi, styled Boyang, came from Wan in Nanyang.

sentences.zh: 少有節操。

translations.literal: Young had integrity conduct.
translations.idiomatic: Even as a youth he showed moral fiber.

sentences.zh: 從兄為人所殺，無子，憙年十五，常思報之。

translations.literal: Cousin elder brother by people killed—without son—Xi age fifteen—constantly thought avenge it.
translations.idiomatic: When his cousin was murdered leaving no heir, fifteen-year-old Zhao plotted revenge.

sentences.zh: 乃挾兵結客，後遂往復仇。

translations.literal: Thereupon carried weapons bound guests—afterward then went take revenge.
translations.idiomatic: He armed himself and gathered allies to settle the score.

sentences.zh: 而仇家皆疾病，無相距者。

translations.literal: Yet rival households all illness—none resisting.
translations.idiomatic: But his foes fell sick—none could fight.

sentences.zh: 憙以因疾報殺，非仁者心，且釋之而去。

translations.literal: Xi thought using illness report kill—not humane person's heart—moreover released them left.
translations.idiomatic: Slaying the sick seemed wrong—he withdrew.

sentences.zh: 顧謂仇曰：『爾曹若健，遠相避也。

translations.literal: Looked told foes saying, "You lot if healthy—far mutually avoid.
translations.idiomatic: He told them: "When you recover—flee far from me."

sentences.zh: 』仇皆臥自搏。

translations.literal: " Foes all lay self striking.
translations.idiomatic: They thrashed helplessly in bed.

sentences.zh: 後病愈，悉自縛詣憙，憙不與相見，後竟殺之。

translations.literal: After illness healed—all self bound went to Xi—Xi not gave mutual see—after finally killed them.
translations.idiomatic: When recovered they bound themselves to Zhao—who refused audience—and later executed them.

sentences.zh: 更始即位，舞陰大姓李氏擁城不下，更始遣柱天將軍李寶降之，不肯，雲：『聞宛之趙氏有孤孫憙，信義著名，願得降之。

translations.literal: Gengshi ascended throne—Wuyin great clan Li clan held city not down—Gengshi dispatched Pillar Heaven General Li Bao surrender them—not willing—said, "Heard Wan's Zhao clan has orphan grandson Xi—trust righteousness famed—wish obtain surrender him.
translations.idiomatic: When Gengshi ruled Wuyin's Li clan held out until they agreed to yield only to Zhao Xi of Wan famed for integrity.

sentences.zh: 』更始乃徵憙。

translations.literal: " Gengshi then summoned Xi.
translations.idiomatic: Gengshi summoned Zhao Xi.

sentences.zh: 憙年未二十，既引見，更始笑曰：『繭栗犢，豈能負重致遠乎？

translations.literal: Xi age not yet twenty—already led audience—Gengshi laughed saying, "Silkworm-chest calf—how can bear heavy reach far?
translations.idiomatic: Under twenty when introduced he was mocked as a yearling calf unfit for heavy burdens.

sentences.zh: 』即除為郎中，行偏將軍事，使詣舞陰，而李氏遂降。

translations.literal: " Immediately appointed Gentleman—conducted lieutenant general affairs—sent reach Wuyin—and Li clan then surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: Yet they named him Gentleman lieutenant general—sent to Wuyin—and the Li surrendered.

sentences.zh: 憙因進入潁川，擊諸不下者，歷汝南界，還宛。

translations.literal: Xi thereby advanced entered Yingchuan—struck various not down—passed Ru'nan border—returned Wan.
translations.idiomatic: He campaigned through Yingchuan and Ru'nan and returned to Wan.

sentences.zh: 更始大悅，謂憙曰：『卿名家駒，努力勉之。

translations.literal: Gengshi greatly pleased—told Xi saying, "You famed stable colt—strive exert yourself.
translations.idiomatic: Gengshi called him a prized colt and urged him onward.

sentences.zh: 』會王莽遣王尋、王邑將兵出關，更始乃拜憙為五威偏將軍，使助諸將拒尋、邑於昆陽。

translations.literal: " Happened Wang Mang dispatched Wang Xun Wang Yi lead troops exit passes—Gengshi then appointed Xi Five Might lieutenant general—sent aid various generals resist Xun Yi at Kunyang.
translations.idiomatic: When Wang Mang sent Wang Xun and Wang Yi out of the passes Gengshi named Zhao lieutenant general to meet them at Kunyang.

sentences.zh: 光武破尋、邑，憙被創，有戰勞，還拜中郎將，封勇功侯。

translations.literal: Guangwu defeated Xun Yi—Xi received wound—had battle toil—returned appointed Leader of Gentlemen—enfeoffed Valorous Merit Marquis.
translations.idiomatic: Wounded at Kunyang Zhao earned the title Leader of Gentlemen and Valorous Merit Marquis.

sentences.zh: 更始敗，憙為赤眉兵所圍，迫急，乃逾屋亡走，與所友善韓仲伯等數十人，攜小弱，越山阻，徑出武關。

translations.literal: Gengshi defeated—Xi by Red Eyebrow troops surrounded—pressed urgent—thereupon crossed roof fled—with friendly Han Zhongbo etc. several tens—carrying small weak—crossed mountain barriers—direct exited Wu Pass.
translations.idiomatic: When Gengshi fell Zhao fled Red Eyebrow siege over rooftops with Han Zhongbo—women and children—over mountains through Wu Pass.

sentences.zh: 仲伯以婦色美，慮有強暴者，而已受其害，欲棄之於道。

translations.literal: Zhongbo because wife color beautiful—feared having violent rapists—yet self receive harm—wished abandon her on road.
translations.idiomatic: Han Zhongbo wished to abandon his beautiful wife on the road for fear of bandits.

sentences.zh: 憙責怒不聽，因以泥塗伯仲婦面，載以鹿車，身自推之。

translations.literal: Xi blamed angry not listen—thereupon with mud smeared Zhongbo wife face—carried with deer cart—body self pushed it.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao refused—smeared mud on her face—hauled her in a handcart himself.

sentences.zh: 每道逢賊，或欲逼略，憙輒言其病狀，以此得免。

translations.literal: Every road meet bandits—or wished force seize—Xi always stated her sick condition—by this obtained exemption.
translations.idiomatic: When bandits threatened he claimed she was ill—sparing her.

sentences.zh: 既入丹水，遇更始親屬，皆裸跣塗炭，饑困不能前。

translations.literal: Already entered Danshui—met Gengshi kin—all naked barefoot smeared charcoal—hungry exhausted unable advance.
translations.idiomatic: In Danshui they met Gengshi kin—filthy and starving.

sentences.zh: 憙見之悲感，所裝縑制資糧，悉以與之，將護歸鄉裏。

translations.literal: Xi seeing them grief moved—what carried silk provisions grain goods—all gave them—led protected return village hamlets.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao gave them his silk provisions and escorted them home.

sentences.zh: 時，鄧奉反於南陽，憙素與奉善，數遺書切責之，而讒者因方憙與奉合謀，帝以為疑。

translations.literal: At time Deng Feng rebelled at Nanyang—Xi always with Feng good—several times sent letters harshly blamed him—yet slanderers thereby figured Xi with Feng conspired—emperor thought suspicious.
translations.idiomatic: When Deng Feng rebelled Zhao wrote urging surrender—slanderers claimed conspiracy—Guangwu doubted him.

sentences.zh: 及奉敗，帝得憙書，乃驚曰：『趙憙真長者也。

translations.literal: When Feng defeated—emperor obtained Xi's letters—then startled said, "Zhao Xi truly mature man.
translations.idiomatic: After Deng fell Guangwu read Zhao's letters and cried him a true gentleman.

sentences.zh: 』即徵憙，引見，賜鞍馬，待詔公車。

translations.literal: " Immediately summoned Xi—led audience—granted saddle horse—await edict Waiting Coaches.
translations.idiomatic: He summoned Zhao—gifted horses—and kept him at the Waiting Coaches.

sentences.zh: 時，江南未賓，道路不通，以憙守簡陽侯相。

translations.literal: At time south of river not guest—roads not passable—ordered Xi guard Jianyang marquis chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: With the south unruly Zhao was named chancellor of Jianyang.

sentences.zh: 憙不肯受兵，單車馳之簡陽。

translations.literal: Xi refused accept troops—single cart galloped there Jianyang.
translations.idiomatic: He refused troops and drove alone to Jianyang.

sentences.zh: 吏民不欲內憙憙，憙乃告譬，呼城中大人，示以國家威信，其帥即開門面縛自歸，由是諸營壁悉降。

translations.literal: Clerks people did not wish admit Xi—Xi then announced analogy—called city inside elders—displayed state awe trust—their chief then opened gate face bound self surrendered—thereby various camps walls all surrendered.
translations.idiomatic: Locals barred him until he persuaded town elders with imperial authority—the chief opened the gates bound—and every stockade surrendered.

sentences.zh: 荊州牧奏憙才任理劇，詔以為平林侯相。

translations.literal: Jing provincial shepherd memorialized Xi talent fit manage acute—ordered deem Pinglin marquis chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: The governor recommended him for tough posts—named chancellor of Pinglin.

sentences.zh: 攻擊群賊，安集已降者，縣邑平定。

translations.literal: Attacked struck bandit hosts—pacified gathered already surrendered—counties towns pacified settled.
translations.idiomatic: He crushed bandits and settled surrendered districts.

sentences.zh: 後拜懷令。

translations.literal: Later appointed Huai magistrate.
translations.idiomatic: Later he became magistrate of Huai.

sentences.zh: 大姓李子春先為瑯邪相，豪猾並兼，為人所患。

translations.literal: Great clan Li Zichun formerly was Langya chancellor—powerful crafty annexed—by people suffered.
translations.idiomatic: Magnate Li Zichun once ruled Langya—predatory and feared.

sentences.zh: 憙下車，聞其二孫殺人事未發覺，即窮詰其奸，收考子春，二孫自殺。

translations.literal: Xi dismounted carriage—heard his two grandsons murder affair not yet discovered—immediately exhaust-challenged their wickedness—seized examined Zichun—two grandsons self-killed.
translations.idiomatic: When Xi took office he learned that his grandsons had committed a murder not yet reported; he pressed the case, arrested and interrogated Li Zichun, and the two grandsons killed themselves.

sentences.zh: 京師為請者數十，終不聽。

translations.literal: Capital for plead dozens—finally not listened.
translations.idiomatic: Dozens of people at the capital pleaded on Li's behalf, yet Xi never yielded.

sentences.zh: 時，趙王良疾病將終，車駕親臨王，問所欲言。

translations.literal: At-that-time Prince Zhao Liang illness disease about-to end—imperial carriage personally arrived prince—asked what wished speak.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Zhao Liang lay dying; the emperor went to him in person and asked what he wished to say.

sentences.zh: 王曰：『素與李子春厚，今犯罪，懷令趙憙欲殺之，願乞其命。

translations.literal: Prince said—always with Li Zichun thick—now commits crime—Huai magistrate Zhao Xi wishes kill him—beg spare his life.
translations.idiomatic: The prince said: "I have long been close to Li Zichun; now he has broken the law, and Grand Administrator Zhao Xi of Huai means to execute him—I beg you to spare his life."

sentences.zh: 』帝曰：『吏奉法，律不可枉也，更道它所欲。

translations.literal: Emperor said—clerks uphold law—statutes cannot bend—further speak other what desires.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor replied: "Officials enforce the law—the code cannot be bent. Tell me anything else you want."

sentences.zh: 』王無復言。

translations.literal: Prince no further words.
translations.idiomatic: The prince said nothing more.

sentences.zh: 既薨，帝追感趙王，乃貰出子春。

translations.literal: Already died—emperor chase-feel Prince Zhao—thereupon pardon-out Zichun.
translations.idiomatic: After the prince died, the emperor, moved by his memory, pardoned Zichun and released him.

sentences.zh: 其年，遷憙平原太守。

translations.literal: That year—transfer Xi Pingyuan grand administrator.
translations.idiomatic: That year Xi was transferred to grand administrator of Pingyuan.

sentences.zh: 時，平原多盜賊，憙與諸郡討捕，斬其渠帥，餘黨當坐者數千人。

translations.literal: At-that-time Pingyuan many bandits-thieves—Xi with various commanderies strike-capture—beheaded their channel-chiefs—remainder faction ought sit several thousand people.
translations.idiomatic: Bandits were rife in Pingyuan; Xi joined other commanderies in suppressing them, executed their ringleaders, and several thousand accomplices faced punishment.

sentences.zh: 憙上言：『惡惡止其身，可一切徙京師近郡。

translations.literal: Xi submitted saying—hate evil stop at his person—may entirely move capital-near commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: Xi memorialized: "Punish the wicked in their own persons only—move all of them en masse to commanderies near the capital."

sentences.zh: 』帝從之，乃悉移置潁川、陳留。

translations.literal: Emperor followed—thereupon entirely shift-place Yingchuan Chenliu.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor agreed and resettled them all in Yingchuan and Chenliu.

sentences.zh: 於是擢舉義行，誅鋤奸惡。

translations.literal: Thereupon promote-select righteous conduct—punish-cut wicked evil.
translations.idiomatic: He then promoted the worthy and stamped out villainy.

sentences.zh: 後青州大蝗，侵入平原界輒死，歲屢有年，百姓歌之。

translations.literal: Later Qingzhou great locusts—invaded-enter Pingyuan border then died—year repeatedly had harvest—hundred surnames sang it.
translations.idiomatic: Later, when Qingzhou was overrun by locusts, any swarm that crossed into Pingyuan died; year after year the harvest held, and the people sang his praises.

sentences.zh: 二十六年，帝延集內戚宴會，歡甚，諸夫人各各前言『趙憙篤義多恩，往遭赤眉出長安，皆為憙所濟活』。

translations.literal: Twenty-sixth year—emperor extended-gather inner kin banquet—joy very—various ladies each each forward said—Zhao Xi firm righteousness much grace—past encountered Red Eyebrows exit Chang'an—all by Xi rescued-alive.
translations.idiomatic: In the twenty-sixth year the emperor hosted a banquet for the imperial in-laws; spirits ran high, and each of the consorts spoke up in turn: "Zhao Xi was steadfast in duty and deep in kindness—when we fled the Red Eyebrows from Chang'an, he saved every one of us."

sentences.zh: 帝甚嘉之。

translations.literal: Emperor greatly praised it.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor was deeply pleased.

sentences.zh: 後徵憙入為太僕，引見謂曰：『卿非但為英雄所保也，婦人亦懷卿之恩。

translations.literal: Later summon Xi enter as grand coachman—introduce-see said—you not-only by heroes protected—women also cherish your grace.
translations.idiomatic: Later Xi was summoned as grand coachman of the household; at audience the emperor told him, "You have been shielded not only by fighting men—the women too owe you their lives."

sentences.zh: 』厚加賞賜。

translations.literal: Thickly added rewards gifts.
translations.idiomatic: He lavished gifts upon him.

sentences.zh: 二十七年，拜太尉，賜爵關內侯。

translations.literal: Twenty-seventh year—appointed grand commandant—bestowed noble rank marquis within-the-passes.
translations.idiomatic: In the twenty-seventh year he was appointed grand commandant and ennobled as marquis within the passes.

sentences.zh: 時，南單於稱臣，烏桓、鮮插並來入朝，帝令憙典邊事，思為久長規。

translations.literal: At-that-time Southern Shanyu called-subject—Wuhuan Xian-cha both came enter court—emperor ordered Xi oversee border affairs—think make long-lasting plan.
translations.idiomatic: The Southern Shanyu had submitted, and the Wuhuan and Xianbei came to court together; the emperor put Xi in charge of frontier policy and asked him to devise a lasting strategy.

sentences.zh: 憙上復緣邊諸郡，幽、並二州由是而定。

translations.literal: Xi submitted restore border-along various commanderies—You Bing two provinces by-this thereby settled.
translations.idiomatic: Xi proposed securing the frontier commanderies along the border, and Youzhou and Bingzhou were thereby stabilized.

sentences.zh: 建初五年，憙疾病，帝親幸視。

translations.literal: Jianchu fifth year—Xi illness disease—emperor personally arrived inspect.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth year of the Jianchu era Xi fell ill, and the emperor visited him in person.

sentences.zh: 及薨，車駕往臨吊。

translations.literal: When died—imperial carriage went arrive mourn.
translations.idiomatic: When he died the sovereign went to mourn him.

sentences.zh: 時年八十四。

translations.literal: At-age eighty-four.
translations.idiomatic: He was eighty-four.

sentences.zh: 謚曰正侯。

translations.literal: Posthumous-title said upright marquis.
translations.idiomatic: His posthumous title was Marquis Upright.

sentences.zh: 子代嗣，官至越騎校尉。

translations.literal: Son Dai succeeded—office reached colonel of agile cavalry.
translations.idiomatic: His son Dai inherited the fief and rose to colonel of the agile cavalry.

sentences.zh: 永元中，副行征西將軍劉尚征羌，坐事下獄，疾病物故。

translations.literal: Yongyuan within—deputy conduct western campaign general Liu Shang campaign Qiang—sit affair descend prison—illness died.
translations.idiomatic: Under Yongyuan he served under General of the Western Expedition Liu Shang against the Qiang; implicated in an affair he was imprisoned and died of illness.

sentences.zh: 和帝憐之，賜秘器錢布，贈越騎校尉、節鄉侯印綬。

translations.literal: Emperor He pitied him—bestowed secret vessels money cloth—posthumously-granted colonel agile cavalry Jie village marquis seals-ribbons.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor He took pity on him, granted him a burial outfit, cash, and cloth, and posthumously awarded the ranks of colonel of the agile cavalry and marquis of Jie village.

sentences.zh: 子直嗣，官至步兵校尉。

translations.literal: Son Zhi succeeded—office reached colonel of foot soldiers.
translations.idiomatic: His son Zhi succeeded him and rose to colonel of foot soldiers.

sentences.zh: 直卒，子淑嗣，無子，國除。

translations.literal: Zhi died—son Shu succeeded—no son—state abolished.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhi died, his son Shu inherited the title; Shu had no heir, and the fief was revoked.

sentences.zh: 牟融字子優，北海安丘人也。

translations.literal: Mou Rong courtesy name Ziyou—Beihai Anqiu person.
translations.idiomatic: Mou Rong, styled Ziyou, was a native of Anqiu in Beihai.

sentences.zh: 少博學，以《大夏侯尚書》教授，門徒數百人，名稱州裏。

translations.literal: Youth broadly learned—with Great Xia Hou Documents instruct—disciples several hundred people—name praised within province.
translations.idiomatic: In youth he was broadly learned; he taught the Great Xia Hou version of the Documents and had hundreds of students, and his reputation ran through the commandery.

sentences.zh: 以司徒茂才為豐令，視事三年，縣無獄訟，為州郡最。

translations.literal: By minister of education abundant-talent became Feng magistrate—administered affairs three years—county no prison lawsuits—was province commandery foremost.
translations.idiomatic: Recommended as an abundant talent by the minister of education, he became magistrate of Feng; in three years on duty the county had no litigation and ranked first in the province.

sentences.zh: 司徒範遷薦融忠正公方，經行純備，宜在本朝，並上其理狀。

translations.literal: Minister Fan Qian recommended Rong loyal upright public square—classic conduct pure complete—ought at this court—and submitted his managing condition.
translations.idiomatic: Minister of Education Fan Qian recommended Rong as loyal, upright, and even-handed, his classical learning and conduct fully rounded, fit for central appointment, and filed a full evaluation.

sentences.zh: 永平五年，入代鮑昱為司隸校尉，多所舉正，百僚敬憚之。

translations.literal: Yongping fifth year—entered replace Bao Yu as metropolitan commandant—many impeached corrected—hundred officials reverent feared him.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth year of Yongping he entered office as metropolitan commandant in succession to Bao Yu; he censored many wrongdoings, and the bureaucracy respected and feared him.

sentences.zh: 八年，代包鹹為大鴻臚。

translations.literal: Eighth year—replaced Bao Xian as grand herald.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth year he succeeded Bao Xian as grand herald.

sentences.zh: 十一年，代鮭陽鴻為大司農。

translations.literal: Eleventh year—replaced Guiyang Hong as grand minister of agriculture.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh year he succeeded Guiyang Hong as grand minister of agriculture.

sentences.zh: 是時，顯宗方勤萬機，公卿數朝會，每輒延謀政事，判折獄訟。

translations.literal: At-this-time Manifest Ancestor just diligent myriad pivots—dukes ministers repeatedly court-meeting—each then extend plot government affairs—judge break prison lawsuits.
translations.idiomatic: At that time Emperor Ming was diligent in every duty; the high ministers met often at court, were repeatedly summoned to deliberate policy, and settled lawsuits.

sentences.zh: 融經明才高，善論議，朝廷皆服其能；

translations.literal: Rong classics bright talent high—good discuss debate—court all submit his ability.
translations.idiomatic: Rong's mastery of the classics and eloquence were outstanding; the court acknowledged his ability.

sentences.zh: 帝數嗟嘆，以為才堪宰相。

translations.literal: Emperor repeatedly sigh-praise—thought talent could grand tutor.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor often sighed his praise, deeming his talent fit for the chancellorship.

sentences.zh: 明年，代伏恭為司空，舉動方重，甚得大臣節。

translations.literal: Next year—replace Fu Gong as minister of works—conduct movements square heavy—greatly obtained great ministers integrity.
translations.idiomatic: The following year he succeeded Fu Gong as minister of works; his bearing was grave and steady, and he fully embodied a senior minister's dignity.

sentences.zh: 肅宗即位，以融先朝名臣，代趙憙為太尉，與憙參錄尚書事。

translations.literal: Suzong ascended throne—because Rong former court famous minister—replace Zhao Xi as grand commandant—with Xi jointly participate record Documents affairs.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor Zhang took the throne he treated Rong as a veteran statesman of the previous reign; Rong succeeded Zhao Xi as grand commandant and joined Xi in supervising the affairs recorded by the Masters of Writing.

sentences.zh: 建初四年薨，車駕親臨其喪。

translations.literal: Jianchu fourth year died—imperial carriage personally arrived his mourning.
translations.idiomatic: He died in the fourth year of Jianchu, and the sovereign attended his funeral in person.

sentences.zh: 時融長子麟歸鄉裏，帝以其餘子幼弱，敕太尉掾史教其威儀進止，贈赗恩寵篤密焉。

translations.literal: At-that-time Rong eldest son Lin returned village—emperor because his remaining sons young weak—ordered grand commandant clerks-teach his awe bearing advance stop—burial gifts grace favor thick dense.
translations.idiomatic: Rong's eldest son Lin was away in his home district; because the younger sons were still small, the emperor ordered the grand commandant's staff to instruct them in deportment and ritual movement, and burial gifts and imperial favor were unusually generous.

sentences.zh: 又賜冢塋地於顯節陵下，除麟為郎。

translations.literal: Further bestowed tomb burial-ground at Manifest Integrity Mausoleum below—appoint Lin as gentleman.
translations.idiomatic: He also granted a burial plot below the Manifest Integrity Mausoleum and appointed Lin as a gentleman-at-court.

sentences.zh: 韋彪字孟達，扶風平陵人也。

translations.literal: Wei Biao courtesy name Mengda—Fufeng Pingling person.
translations.idiomatic: Wei Biao, styled Mengda, was a native of Pingling in Fufeng.

sentences.zh: 高祖賢，宣帝時為丞相。

translations.literal: High-ancestor Xian—Emperor Xuan-time was chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: His ancestor Wei Xian served as chancellor under Emperor Xuan.

sentences.zh: 祖賞，哀帝時為大司馬。

translations.literal: Grandfather Shang—Emperor Ai-time was grand marshal.
translations.idiomatic: His grandfather Wei Shang was grand marshal under Emperor Ai.

sentences.zh: 彪孝行純至，父母卒，哀毀三年，不出廬寢。

translations.literal: Biao filial conduct pure utmost—father mother died—grief waste three years—not exit hut sleeping-quarters.
translations.idiomatic: Biao's filial devotion was absolute; when his parents died he mourned until he was wasted with grief for three years and did not leave the mourning hut.

sentences.zh: 服竟，贏瘠骨立異形，醫療數年乃起。

translations.literal: Mourning finished—emaciated lean bone stand strange form—medical treat several years then rose.
translations.idiomatic: When mourning ended he was gaunt to the bone and barely recognizable; only after years of care could he rise again.

sentences.zh: 好學洽聞，雅稱儒宗。

translations.literal: Good study broad-hear—elegantly titled Confucian patriarch.
translations.idiomatic: He loved learning and was widely read, and was acclaimed as a Confucian master.

sentences.zh: 建武末，舉孝廉，除郎中，以病免，復歸教授。

translations.literal: Jianwu end—raised filial incorrupt—appoint gentleman-of-the-palace—by illness dismissed—again return instruct.
translations.idiomatic: At the end of the Jianwu era he was recommended as filial and incorrupt, appointed gentleman of the palace, retired on grounds of illness, and returned to teaching.

sentences.zh: 安貧樂道，恬於進趣，三輔諸儒莫不慕仰之。

translations.literal: Peace poverty delight Way—quiet at advance ambition—three assistants various Confucians none-not admire look-up him.
translations.idiomatic: Content in poverty and devoted to the Way, indifferent to advancement, he was admired by every scholar of the capital region.

sentences.zh: 建初七年，車駕西巡府，以彪行太常從，數召入，問以三輔舊事，禮儀風俗。

translations.literal: Jianchu seventh year—imperial carriage west tour prefecture—because Biao act grand astrologer follow—several summon enter—ask by three assistants old affairs—rites etiquette wind customs.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh year of Jianchu the sovereign toured west to the former capital; Biao accompanied him as acting grand astrologer and was summoned repeatedly to speak on old customs, ritual, and usage in the capital region.

sentences.zh: 彪因建言：『今西巡舊都，宜追錄高祖、中宗功臣、褒顯先勛，紀其子孫。

translations.literal: Biao therefore submitted saying—now west tour old capital—ought pursue record Gaozu Zhongzong merit ministers—praise display former merit—record their descendants.
translations.idiomatic: Biao therefore proposed: "On this western tour of the former capital we should trace and honor the founding ministers of Gaozu and Zhongzong, celebrate their deeds, and register their descendants."

sentences.zh: 』帝納之。

translations.literal: Emperor accepted it.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor adopted his advice.

sentences.zh: 行至長安，乃制詔京兆尹、右扶風求蕭何、霍光後。

translations.literal: Walk arrived Chang'an—thereupon decree Metropolitan Governor Right Fufeng seek Xiao He Huo Guang posterity.
translations.idiomatic: When they reached Chang'an he issued edicts to the metropolitan governor and the governor of Right Fufeng to seek descendants of Xiao He and Huo Guang.

sentences.zh: 時光無苗裔，唯封何末孫熊為酂侯。

translations.literal: At-that-time Guang had no descendants—only enfeoff He's last-grandson Xiong as Zan marquis.
translations.idiomatic: Huo Guang had no surviving line; only Xiao He's last descendant Xiong was enfeoffed as marquis of Zan.

sentences.zh: 建初二年已封曹參後曹湛為平陽侯，故不復及焉。

translations.literal: Jianchu second year already enfeoffed Cao Can posterity Cao Zhan as Pingyang marquis—therefore not again reach.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year of Jianchu Cao Zhan, a descendant of Cao Can, had already been made marquis of Pingyang, so he was not mentioned again.

sentences.zh: 乃厚賜彪錢珍羞食物，使歸平陵上冢。

translations.literal: Thereupon thick bestow Biao money rare delicacies food—cause return Pingling ascend tomb.
translations.idiomatic: Biao was richly rewarded with cash and fine provisions and sent home to Pingling to sacrifice at the ancestral graves.

sentences.zh: 還，拜大鴻臚。

translations.literal: Returned—appointed grand herald.
translations.idiomatic: On his return he was appointed grand herald.

sentences.zh: 是時，陳事者多言郡國貢舉率非功次，故守職益懈而吏事浸疏，咎在州郡。

translations.literal: At-this-time submit-affairs many speak commandery-state tribute selections generally not merit order—therefore guard duty increasingly slack and clerk affairs gradually sparse—fault lies province commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Petitioners were saying that recommendations from the commanderies seldom followed order of merit, so officials grew lax and administration drifted—and blame lay with the provincial governors.

sentences.zh: 有詔下公卿朝臣議。

translations.literal: Had edict descend dukes ministers court ministers deliberate.
translations.idiomatic: An edict went out ordering the high ministers and court officials to debate the matter.

sentences.zh: 彪上議曰：『伏惟明詔，憂勞百姓，垂恩選舉，務得其人。

translations.literal: Biao submitted deliberation saying—prostrate think bright edict—worry toil hundred surnames—hang grace selection-raising—strive obtain its person.
translations.idiomatic: Biao presented his opinion: "Your enlightened edict shows concern for the people and grace in choosing officials—we must secure the right men."

sentences.zh: 夫國以簡賢為務，賢以孝行為首。

translations.literal: Now state by select worthy as task—worthy by filial conduct as head.
translations.idiomatic: The state's task is to choose worthies, and worthies are known first by filial conduct.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰：「事親孝故忠可移於君，是以求忠臣必於孝子之門。

translations.literal: Confucius said—serve kin filial therefore loyalty can shift to lord—thereby seek loyal ministers must at filial sons' gates.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius said, "When one is filial at home, loyalty to the ruler follows—so one seeks loyal ministers among filial sons."

sentences.zh: 」夫人才行少能相兼，是以孟公綽優於趙、魏老，不可以為滕、薛大夫。

translations.literal: Now person talent conduct few can mutually combine—thereby Meng Gongchuo excel at Zhao Wei elder—not can be Teng Xue grandee.
translations.idiomatic: Talents rarely excel in every role—hence Meng Gongchuo was suited as steward to the great houses of Zhao and Wei but not as minister of petty states such as Teng or Xue.

sentences.zh: 忠孝之人，持心近厚；

translations.literal: Loyal filial people—hold heart near thick.
translations.idiomatic: Loyal and filial men keep generous hearts.

sentences.zh: 鍛煉之吏，持心近薄。

translations.literal: Forge-refine clerks—hold heart near thin.
translations.idiomatic: Harsh, manipulative clerks incline to cruelty.

sentences.zh: 三代之所以直道而行者，在其所以磨之故也。

translations.literal: Three generations' reason straight Way but walk—at their reason grind it.
translations.idiomatic: The reason the Three Ages could walk the straight path lay in how they tempered character.

sentences.zh: 士宜以才行為先，不可純以閥閱。

translations.literal: Scholar ought by talent conduct as first—not may purely by pedigree.
translations.idiomatic: Scholars should be judged first on talent and conduct, not solely on pedigree.

sentences.zh: 然其要歸，在於選二千石。

translations.literal: Yet its essential return—lies at select two-thousand piculs.
translations.idiomatic: Yet the crux lies in choosing the grand administrators.

sentences.zh: 二千石賢，則貢舉皆得其人矣。

translations.literal: Two-thousand piculs worthy—then tribute selections all obtain its person.
translations.idiomatic: When the grand administrators are worthy, every recommendation yields the right man.

sentences.zh: 』帝深納之。

translations.literal: Emperor deeply accepted it.
translations.idiomatic: The emperor embraced his advice wholeheartedly.

sentences.zh: 彪以世承二帝吏化之後，多以苛刻為能，又置官選職，不必以才，因盛夏多寒，上疏諫曰：『臣聞政化之本，必順陰陽。

translations.literal: Biao because generation inherit two emperors clerk transform after—many by harsh demanding as ability—further place official select office—not must by talent—because flourishing summer many cold—submit memorial remonstrate said—your servant hear government transform root—must comply yin-yang.
translations.idiomatic: Biao observed that after two reigns of bureaucratic rule many took harshness for competence, and appointments did not always rest on talent; because midsummer had turned unseasonably cold, he submitted a memorial: "I have heard that the root of good government lies in harmonizing yin and yang."

sentences.zh: 伏見立夏以來，當暑而寒，殆以刑罰刻急，郡國不奉時令之所致也。

translations.literal: Prostrate see establish summer since—ought heat yet cold—perhaps by punishments carve urgent—commandery-states not obey seasons commands caused.
translations.idiomatic: Since the Beginning of Summer we have had cold when heat was due—surely because punishments are severe and the commanderies ignore the seasons' commands.

sentences.zh: 農人急於務而苛吏奪其時，賦發充常調而貪吏割其財，此其巨患也。

translations.literal: Farmers urgent at tasks yet harsh clerks seize their times—tax levies fill constant levies yet greedy clerks cut their wealth—this its great calamity.
translations.idiomatic: Farmers rush their work while petty clerks steal their seasons; taxes meet routine quotas while grasping officers bleed their wealth—these are grave ills.

sentences.zh: 夫欲急人所務，當先除其所患。

translations.literal: Now desire hurry people what pursue—ought first remove what they suffer.
translations.idiomatic: To hasten what the people must do, first remove what harms them.

sentences.zh: 天下樞要，在於尚書，尚書之選，豈可不重？

translations.literal: Under-heaven pivot crucial—lies at Masters of Writing—Masters of Writing selection—how can not heavy.
translations.idiomatic: The pivot of the realm is the Masters of Writing; can their selection be treated lightly?

sentences.zh: 而間者多從郎官超升此位，雖曉習文法，長於應對，然察察小慧，類無大能。

translations.literal: Yet among many from gentleman-of-the-palace leap rise this position—although dawn-practice statutes—excel at respond-reply—yet nitpick small cleverness—class lack great ability.
translations.idiomatic: Among those promoted many vault from gentlemen of the palace: they know the letter of the law and shine in repartee but show petty cleverness and seldom great capacity.

sentences.zh: 宜簡嘗歷州宰素有名者，雖進退舒遲，時有不逮，然端心向公，奉職周密。

translations.literal: Ought select once experienced province magistrate plain have fame—although advance retreat slow delay—times have not reach—yet upright heart face public—uphold duty thorough dense.
translations.idiomatic: Choose men who have governed commanderies and earned repute; though slow to advance or retreat and sometimes fall short, they are upright, devoted to the public trust, and conscientious.

sentences.zh: 宜鑒嗇夫捷急之對，沈思絳侯木訥之功也。

translations.literal: Ought mirror granary-man quick-urgent reply—deep ponder Jiang marquis wooden-tongued merit.
translations.idiomatic: Take warning from the petty granary clerk's glib answer; weigh instead the slow, solid worth of the Marquis of Jiang.

sentences.zh: 往時楚獄大起，故置令史以助郎職，而類多小人，好為奸利。

translations.literal: Past times Chu prisons greatly arose—therefore place clerks to assist gentleman duties—yet type many petty men—like act wicked profit.
translations.idiomatic: When the Chu witchcraft prosecutions flared, clerks were added to aid the gentlemen of the palace—yet most were mean-spirited men who chased illicit gain.

sentences.zh: 今者務簡，可皆停省。

translations.literal: Now affairs strive simplicity—may all stop eliminate.
translations.idiomatic: Today policy stresses simplicity; these posts may all be abolished.

sentences.zh: 又諫議之職，應用公直之士，通財謇正，有補益於朝者。

translations.literal: Further remonstrance deliberation duty—ought employ public upright scholars—penetrate wealth halt upright—have supplement benefit at court.
translations.idiomatic: The office of remonstrance should be filled with upright men—frank counselors of integrity who strengthen the court.

sentences.zh: 今或從征試輩為大夫。

translations.literal: Now some follow examination-test cohorts become grandees.
translations.idiomatic: Some are now advanced from examination cohorts straight to grandee rank.

sentences.zh: 又御史外遷，動據州郡。

translations.literal: Further imperial clerks outside transfer—always seize province commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: Imperial clerks rotated into the provinces routinely take commandery posts.

sentences.zh: 並宜清選其任，責以言績。

translations.literal: Both ought pure-select their appointments—blame by words achievement.
translations.idiomatic: In every case choose appointees with care and hold them accountable for what they promise and what they deliver.

sentences.zh: 其二千石視事雖久，而為吏民所便安者，宜增秩重賞，勿妄遷徙。

translations.literal: Those two-thousand piculs administer affairs although long—yet by clerks people convenient peace—ought increase rank heavy reward—not rashly transfer.
translations.idiomatic: Grand administrators long in office whom officials and commoners find reliable should receive higher rank and generous rewards—do not shuffle them about without cause.

sentences.zh: 惟留聖心。

translations.literal: Only leave sagely heart.
translations.idiomatic: I leave this to Your Majesty's wise judgment.

sentences.zh: 』書奏，帝納之。

translations.literal: Memorial submitted—emperor accepted it.
translations.idiomatic: The memorial was presented and the emperor adopted it.

sentences.zh: 元和二年春，東巡狩，以彪行司徒事從行。

translations.literal: Yuanhe second year spring—east tour hunt—because Biao act minister of education affairs follow-go.
translations.idiomatic: In the spring of the second Yuanhe year the sovereign hunted eastward; Biao accompanied him, acting as minister of education.

sentences.zh: 還，以病乞身，帝遣小黃門、太醫問病，賜以食物。

translations.literal: Returned—by illness beg body—emperor dispatched minor yellow gate grand physician ask illness—bestow by food.
translations.idiomatic: On his return he asked to retire on grounds of ill health; the emperor sent a minor yellow-gate attendant and the imperial physician to inquire after him and granted food.

sentences.zh: 彪遂稱困篤。

translations.literal: Biao thereupon declared distressed grave.
translations.idiomatic: Biao then declared himself gravely ill.

sentences.zh: 章和二年夏，使謁者策詔曰：『彪以將相之裔，勤身飭行，出自州裏，在位歷載。

translations.literal: Zhanghe second year summer—cause herald bamboo-edict said—Biao by general minister posterity—diligent body adorn conduct—emerge from province village—at position pass years.
translations.idiomatic: In the summer of the second Zhanghe year a herald delivered an edict on bamboo slips: "Biao, though sprung from a line of generals and ministers, has disciplined himself and risen from the provinces to serve many years."

sentences.zh: 中被篤疾，連上求退。

translations.literal: Middle struck grave illness—repeatedly submitted seek retire.
translations.idiomatic: Stricken by serious illness, he has repeatedly asked permission to withdraw.

sentences.zh: 君年在耆艾，不可復以加增，恐職事煩碎，重有損焉。

translations.literal: Lord age at aged-grey—not may again by add increase—fear duties vexed fragment—again have harm.
translations.idiomatic: You are already of venerable age and cannot bear heavier burdens; I fear petty duties would only harm you further.

sentences.zh: 其上大鴻臚印綬。

translations.literal: Submit ascend grand herald seals-ribbons.
translations.idiomatic: Surrender the seals of office as grand herald.

sentences.zh: 其遣太子舍人詣中臧府，受賜錢二十萬。

translations.literal: Cause dispatch heir-apparent attendant-go reach inner treasury—receive bestowed cash two hundred thousand.
translations.idiomatic: Send an attendant of the heir apparent to the inner treasury to receive two hundred thousand cash in gifts.

sentences.zh: 』永元元年，卒，詔尚書：『故大鴻臚韋彪，在位無愆，方欲錄用，奄忽而卒。

translations.literal: Yongyuan first year—died—edict Masters of Writing—former grand herald Wei Biao—at position without fault—just about record employ—suddenly died.
translations.idiomatic: In the first year of Yongyuan he died. An edict to the Masters of Writing ran: "The late grand herald Wei Biao served without fault; we were about to recall him when he passed away suddenly."

sentences.zh: 其賜錢二十萬，布百匹，穀三千斛。』

translations.literal: Cause bestow cash two hundred thousand—cloth hundred bolts—grain three thousand hu.
translations.idiomatic: Grant him two hundred thousand cash, one hundred bolts of cloth, and three thousand hu of grain.

sentences.zh: 彪清儉好施，祿賜分與宗族，家無餘財，著書十二篇，號曰《韋卿子》。

translations.literal: Biao pure frugal fond give—stipend gifts divide-with clan—home no surplus wealth—compose writings twelve chapters—title called Documents Master Wei Minister.
translations.idiomatic: Biao lived plainly and loved to give; he shared salaries and imperial gifts with his kin and kept nothing at home. He wrote twelve essays collected under the title Discourses of Minister Wei.

sentences.zh: 族子義

translations.literal: Clansman Yi.
translations.idiomatic: Yi, a clansman

sentences.zh: 義字季節。

translations.literal: Yi courtesy name Jijie.
translations.idiomatic: Yi, styled Jijie,

sentences.zh: 高祖父玄成，元帝時為丞相。

translations.literal: Great-grandfather Xuancheng—Emperor Yuan-time was chancellor.
translations.idiomatic: His great-grandfather Wei Xuancheng had been chancellor under Emperor Yuan.

sentences.zh: 初，彪獨徙扶風，故義猶為京兆杜陵人焉。

translations.literal: At-first Biao alone moved Fufeng—therefore Yi still was Jingzhao Duling person.
translations.idiomatic: At first only Biao had relocated to Fufeng, so Yi remained registered as a native of Duling in Jingzhao.

sentences.zh: 兄順，字淑文，平輿令。

translations.literal: Elder-brother Shun—courtesy name Shuwen—Pingyu magistrate.
translations.idiomatic: His elder brother Shun, styled Shuwen, was magistrate of Pingyu.

sentences.zh: 有高名。

translations.literal: Had high name.
translations.idiomatic: He enjoyed high renown.

sentences.zh: 次兄豹，字季明。

translations.literal: Next-elder-brother Bao—courtesy name Jiming.
translations.idiomatic: His next elder brother Bao, styled Jiming,

sentences.zh: 數辟公府，輒以事去。

translations.literal: Repeatedly summon duke offices—always by affair leave.
translations.idiomatic: He was summoned repeatedly to serve in high ministers' offices but always bowed out on some excuse.

sentences.zh: 司徒劉愷復辟之，謂曰：『卿以輕好去就，爵位不躋。

translations.literal: Minister Liu Kai again summon him—said—you by light-like go-come—noble rank not ascend.
translations.idiomatic: Minister of Education Liu Kai summoned him again and said: "You treat appointments lightly and move in and out of office at whim—honors have passed you by."

sentences.zh: 今歲垂盡，當選御史，意在相薦，子其宿留乎？

translations.literal: Now year hangs exhausted—ought elect imperial clerk—intent at mutually recommend—you perhaps linger?
translations.idiomatic: The year is nearly done—the imperial clerk selection is due—I mean to recommend you—will you stay on for it?

sentences.zh: 』豹曰：『犬馬齒衰，旅力已劣，仰慕崇恩，故未能自割。

translations.literal: Bao said—dog-horse teeth decay—travel strength already weak—look-up admire lofty grace—therefore not-able self-cut.
translations.idiomatic: Bao replied: "Like an old horse I am failing; I still crave your kindness too dearly to tear myself away from office yet."

sentences.zh: 且眩瞀滯疾，不堪久待，選薦之私，非所敢當。

translations.literal: Moreover dizzy dim stagnant illness—not endure long await—selection recommend private—not dare bear.
translations.idiomatic: Besides, dizzy spells and chronic illness leave me unfit to wait long for appointment—the favor of your nomination is more than I dare accept.

sentences.zh: 』遂跣而起。

translations.literal: Thereupon barefoot rise walk.
translations.idiomatic: With that he rose barefoot.

sentences.zh: 愷追之，徑去不顧。

translations.literal: Kai pursued him—straight go not look-back.
translations.idiomatic: Kai hurried after him, but Bao walked straight away without turning.

sentences.zh: 安帝西巡，徵拜議郎。

translations.literal: Emperor An west tour—summon appoint gentleman-consultant.
translations.idiomatic: When Emperor An toured the west he summoned Wei Yi and appointed him gentleman consultant.

sentences.zh: 義少與二兄齊名，初仕州郡。

translations.literal: Yi youth with two elder-brothers equal-name—first served province commandery.
translations.idiomatic: In youth Yi stood equal in fame with his two elder brothers; he began his career in provincial posts.

sentences.zh: 太傅桓焉辟舉理劇，為廣都長，甘陵、陳二縣令，政甚有績，官曹無事，牢獄空虛。

translations.literal: Grand tutor Huan Yan summon raise manage difficult—became Guangdu chief—Ganling Chen two county magistrates—government very had achievements—official offices no affairs—prisons empty void.
translations.idiomatic: Grand Tutor Huan Yan recommended him for a difficult assignment; he served as chief of Guangdu and as magistrate of Ganling and Chen, earning an outstanding record—his offices ran quietly and the prisons stood empty.

sentences.zh: 數上書順帝，陳宜依古典，考功黜陟，徵集名儒，大定其制。

translations.literal: Repeatedly submitted writings Emperor Shun—declare ought rely ancient norms—examine merit demote promote—gather assemble famous Confucians—great settle its system.
translations.idiomatic: He repeatedly memorialized Emperor Shun, urging adherence to classical precedent in merit ratings and promotions, convening renowned scholars, and firmly settling the regulations.

sentences.zh: 又譏切左右，貶刺竇氏。

translations.literal: Further ridicule-cut left-right—demean stab Dou clan.
translations.idiomatic: He also rebuked those around the throne and satirized the Dou clan.

sentences.zh: 言既無感，而久抑不遷，以兄順喪去官。

translations.literal: Words already without move-feel—yet long repress not transfer—because elder-brother Shun mourning left office.
translations.idiomatic: His words went unheeded, and he languished without promotion; he left office to mourn his elder brother Shun.

sentences.zh: 比辟公府，不就。

translations.literal: When summon duke office—not accept.
translations.idiomatic: When summoned to a minister's office he did not take the post.

sentences.zh: 廣都為生立廟。

translations.literal: Guangdu for living establish temple.
translations.idiomatic: The people of Guangdu built him a shrine while he was still alive.

sentences.zh: 及卒，三縣吏民為義舉哀，若喪考妣。

translations.literal: When died—three counties clerks people for Yi raise grief—as mourn father mother.
translations.idiomatic: When he died, clerks and commoners in all three counties mourned him as they would a parent.

sentences.zh: 豹子著

translations.literal: Bao son Zhu.
translations.idiomatic: Zhu, son of Bao

sentences.zh: 贊曰：『湛、霸奮庸，維寧兩邦。

translations.literal: Praise says—Fu Zhan Wang Ba raise merit—tie pacify two states.
translations.idiomatic: The summation reads: "Fu Zhan and Wang Ba threw their talents into service and brought peace to two domains."

sentences.zh: 淮人孺慕，徐寇要降。

translations.literal: Huai people child-long—Xu bandits invite surrender.
translations.idiomatic: The Huai regions cherished them; the Xu rebels sued for surrender.

sentences.zh: 弘實體遠，仁不忘本。

translations.literal: Hong substance body far—benevolence not forget root.
translations.idiomatic: Song Hong embodied breadth of vision; benevolence never forgot its roots.

sentences.zh: 憙政多跡，彪明理損。

translations.literal: Xi government many traces—Biao bright principle diminish.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Xi left many traces of good rule; Wei Biao clarified principle and curbed excess.

sentences.zh: 牟公簡帝，身終上袞。』

translations.literal: Lord Mou brief emperor—body end upper ceremonial robe.
translations.idiomatic: Lord Mou advised the emperor plainly and died wearing the highest ceremonial robes.
